2026 Iran war  
---  
Part of the [Iran–Israel conflict](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iran%E2%80%93Israel_conflict "Iran–Israel conflict"), [Middle Eastern crisis](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Middle_Eastern_crisis_\(2023%E2%80%93present\) "Middle Eastern crisis \(2023–present\)"), and [American expansionism under Donald Trump](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/American_expansionism_under_Donald_Trump "American expansionism under Donald Trump")  
![Map](https://maps.wikimedia.org/img/osm-intl,4,30,47,300x300.png?lang=en&domain=en.wikipedia.org&title=2026_Iran_war&revid=1346495231&groups=_4040b9d1659f88290d32d5285ea5b96bd2334990&parser=legacy)[Wikimedia](https://foundation.wikimedia.org/wiki/Maps_Terms_of_Use) | © [OpenStreetMap](https://www.openstreetmap.org/copyright)
Locations struck by: 
  *  [![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d2/Maki7-cross-white.svg/20px-Maki7-cross-white.svg.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Maki7-cross-white.svg)  United States and Israel
  *  [![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/fa/Maki7-circle-stroked-white.svg/20px-Maki7-circle-stroked-white.svg.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Maki7-circle-stroked-white.svg)  Iran, [Hezbollah](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hezbollah "Hezbollah"), [Houthis](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Houthis "Houthis"), and [PMF](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Popular_Mobilization_Forces "Popular Mobilization Forces")
  *     * [v](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Template:2026_Iran_war_map "Template:2026 Iran war map")
    * [t](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Template_talk:2026_Iran_war_map "Template talk:2026 Iran war map")
    * [e](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:EditPage/Template:2026_Iran_war_map "Special:EditPage/Template:2026 Iran war map")

  
| Date| 28 February 2026 (2026-02-28) – present  
(1 month and 4 days)  
---|---  
Location| 
  * West Asia[f]
  * Indian Ocean[g]

  
Status| 
  * [Ongoing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_ongoing_armed_conflicts "List of ongoing armed conflicts")


  * Assassination of [top Iranian officials](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Iranian_officials_killed_during_the_2026_Iran_war "List of Iranian officials killed during the 2026 Iran war") in [US–Israeli strikes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_attacks_during_the_2026_Iran_war#Operations_Roaring_Lion_and_Epic_Fury "List of attacks during the 2026 Iran war"), including [Ali Khamenei](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Assassination_of_Ali_Khamenei "Assassination of Ali Khamenei") and [Ali Larijani](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Assassination_of_Ali_Larijani "Assassination of Ali Larijani")
  * [Iranian retaliation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_attacks_during_the_2026_Iran_war#Iranian_response "List of attacks during the 2026 Iran war") by striking [Israel](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iranian_strikes_on_Israel "2026 Iranian strikes on Israel") and regional US allies
  * Establishment of [Iranian control](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Strait_of_Hormuz_crisis "2026 Strait of Hormuz crisis") over the [Strait of Hormuz](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Strait_of_Hormuz "Strait of Hormuz") and [toll](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Toll_\(fee\) "Toll \(fee\)") collection in [Chinese yuan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Renminbi "Renminbi") for [oil](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Crude_oil "Crude oil") sold through the strait[9][10]
  * [Deployment of military assets in Cyprus](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iran_war_regional_mobilizations#In_Cyprus_and_the_Balkans "2026 Iran war regional mobilizations") by some European countries following [a drone attack in the island](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_drone_strikes_on_Akrotiri_and_Dhekelia "2026 drone strikes on Akrotiri and Dhekelia")
  * [Election](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iranian_supreme_leader_election "2026 Iranian supreme leader election") of [Mojtaba Khamenei](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mojtaba_Khamenei "Mojtaba Khamenei") as the [Supreme Leader of Iran](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Supreme_Leader_of_Iran "Supreme Leader of Iran")
  * [Escalation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Lebanon_war "2026 Lebanon war") of the [Israel–Hezbollah conflict](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hezbollah%E2%80%93Israel_conflict_\(2023%E2%80%93present\) "Hezbollah–Israel conflict \(2023–present\)")
  * [Global economic disruption](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Economic_impact_of_the_2026_Iran_war "Economic impact of the 2026 Iran war")

Casualties and losses  
  * **Per US and Israel:**
    * ![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/a/a4/Flag_of_the_United_States.svg/40px-Flag_of_the_United_States.svg.png) United States: 
      * 15 soldiers killed[h][12]
      * 348 military personnel wounded[i]
      * At least 17 US sites[j] in the Middle East damaged[15] equivalent to loss of $800m[16]
      * _For aviation losses, see[this list](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_aviation_shootdowns_and_accidents_during_the_2026_Iran_war "List of aviation shootdowns and accidents during the 2026 Iran war")_
    * ![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d4/Flag_of_Israel.svg/40px-Flag_of_Israel.svg.png) Israel: 
      * 11 soldiers killed[k]
      * 23 civilians killed[l]
      * 6,131 injured[28]
      * 261 military personnel wounded[29]


* * *
  * **Per Iran:**
    * ![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/a/a4/Flag_of_the_United_States.svg/40px-Flag_of_the_United_States.svg.png) United States: 
      * At least 12 [radar systems](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Radar "Radar") and [satellite terminals](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Satcom_\(satellite\) "Satcom \(satellite\)") destroyed or damaged[30]
      * Multiple [AN/TPY-2s](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/AN/TPY-2_transportable_radar "AN/TPY-2 transportable radar") destroyed or damaged[31]
      * 1 [THAAD](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Terminal_High_Altitude_Area_Defense "Terminal High Altitude Area Defense") radar destroyed or seriously damaged[32]
      * 1 [AN/FPS-132](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Solid_State_Phased_Array_Radar_System "Solid State Phased Array Radar System"), 1 [AN/TPS-59](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/AN/FPS-117 "AN/FPS-117")[33], 1 [AN/FPS-117](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/AN/FPS-117 "AN/FPS-117")[34], 1[AN/MPQ-64](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/AN/MPQ-64_Sentinel "AN/MPQ-64 Sentinel") destroyed[35], 1 [MIM-104 Patriot](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/MIM-104_Patriot "MIM-104 Patriot") AN/MPQ-65 radar and 1 [AN/TPS-75](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/AN/TPS-75 "AN/TPS-75") damaged[36]

On 28 February 2026, the [United States](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_States "United States") and [Israel](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Israel "Israel") started an armed conflict with surprise airstrikes on sites and cities across [Iran](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iran "Iran"), [assassinating](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Assassination_of_Ali_Khamenei "Assassination of Ali Khamenei") Supreme Leader [Ali Khamenei](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ali_Khamenei "Ali Khamenei") and [several other Iranian officials](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Iranian_officials_killed_during_the_2026_Iran_war "List of Iranian officials killed during the 2026 Iran war") as well as inflicting dozens of civilian casualties. Iran responded with missile and drone strikes against Israel, [US bases](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_American_military_installations#Foreign_bases_and_facilities "List of American military installations"), and US-allied countries in the [Middle East](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Middle_East "Middle East"). 
After the [Middle Eastern crisis](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Middle_Eastern_crisis_\(2023%E2%80%93present\) "Middle Eastern crisis \(2023–present\)") began in 2023, Iran and Israel exchanged [missile strikes in 2024](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2024_Iran%E2%80%93Israel_conflict "2024 Iran–Israel conflict"), and Israel and the [US launched airstrikes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2025_United_States_strikes_on_Iranian_nuclear_sites "2025 United States strikes on Iranian nuclear sites") against Iran in the [Twelve-Day War](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Twelve-Day_War "Twelve-Day War") in June 2025. In January 2026, Iranian security forces [killed thousands of protesters](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iran_massacres "2026 Iran massacres") in their crackdown of the [largest Iranian protests](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2025%E2%80%932026_Iranian_protests "2025–2026 Iranian protests") since the [1979 Islamic Revolution](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1979_Islamic_Revolution "1979 Islamic Revolution"). US president [Donald Trump](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Donald_Trump "Donald Trump") responded by threatening military action against Iran and launching the [largest US military buildup in the region](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_United_States_military_buildup_in_the_Middle_East "2026 United States military buildup in the Middle East") since the US-led [2003 invasion of Iraq](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2003_invasion_of_Iraq "2003 invasion of Iraq"). In mid-February, the US and Iran began a new round of [indirect nuclear negotiations](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2025%E2%80%932026_Iran%E2%80%93United_States_negotiations "2025–2026 Iran–United States negotiations").[87]
The surprise US-Israeli attack, launched during the nuclear negotiations, killed Khamenei, other Iranian officials, and civilians.[88][89] Subsequent attacks damaged military bases, government facilities, [schools](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Minab_school_attack "2026 Minab school attack"), hospitals, and cultural heritage sites.[90] In retaliation, Iran [launched](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iranian_strikes_on_Israel "2026 Iranian strikes on Israel") hundreds of [drones](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Unmanned_aerial_vehicles_in_the_Iranian_military "Unmanned aerial vehicles in the Iranian military") and [ballistic missiles](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ballistic_missile "Ballistic missile") at targets in Israel and [at US military bases](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iranian_strikes_on_Arab_countries "2026 Iranian strikes on Arab countries") in [Bahrain](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iranian_strikes_on_Bahrain "2026 Iranian strikes on Bahrain"), [Jordan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iranian_strikes_on_Jordan "2026 Iranian strikes on Jordan"), [Kuwait](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iranian_strikes_on_Kuwait "2026 Iranian strikes on Kuwait"), [Qatar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iranian_strikes_on_Qatar "2026 Iranian strikes on Qatar"), [Saudi Arabia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iranian_strikes_on_Saudi_Arabia "2026 Iranian strikes on Saudi Arabia"), and the [United Arab Emirates](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iranian_strikes_on_the_United_Arab_Emirates "2026 Iranian strikes on the United Arab Emirates").[91][92] [A drone struck](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_drone_strikes_on_Akrotiri_and_Dhekelia "2026 drone strikes on Akrotiri and Dhekelia") Britain's [Akrotiri](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Akrotiri_and_Dhekelia "Akrotiri and Dhekelia") military base on [Cyprus](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cyprus_\(island\) "Cyprus \(island\)"), and missiles were [shot down](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_interceptions_of_Iranian_missiles_in_Turkey "2026 interceptions of Iranian missiles in Turkey") over [Turkey](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Turkey "Turkey"). Other strikes hit civilian infrastructure including in [Azerbaijan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iranian_strikes_on_Azerbaijan "2026 Iranian strikes on Azerbaijan"),[93] [Kurdistan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iranian_strikes_on_the_Kurdistan_Region "2026 Iranian strikes on the Kurdistan Region"), and [Oman](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iranian_strikes_on_Oman "2026 Iranian strikes on Oman").[94] Iran denied striking Azerbaijan, Oman, and Turkey, saying they were [false-flag](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/False_flag "False flag") attacks.[95][96] Two ballistic missiles were reportedly launched at [Diego Garcia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Diego_Garcia "Diego Garcia") military base on the [Chagos Islands](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chagos_Islands "Chagos Islands"),[97][98] which Iran also denied.[99] The [conflict between Hezbollah and Israel](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hezbollah%E2%80%93Israel_conflict_\(2023%E2%80%93present\) "Hezbollah–Israel conflict \(2023–present\)") escalated into the [2026 Lebanon war](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Lebanon_war "2026 Lebanon war"), killing more than 1,000 militants and civilians by late March.[100][101][102][103]
[Trump administration](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Second_presidency_of_Donald_Trump "Second presidency of Donald Trump") officials have offered diverse and changing [explanations for starting the war](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rationale_for_the_2026_Iran_war "Rationale for the 2026 Iran war"),[104] such as to pre-empt Iranian retaliation against U.S. assets after an expected Israeli attack on Iran,[105][106][107][108] to ward off an imminent Iranian threat, to destroy [Iran's missile](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ballistic_missile_program_of_Iran "Ballistic missile program of Iran") and [military capabilities](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Military_of_Iran "Military of Iran"), to prevent Iran obtaining a [nuclear weapon](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nuclear_weapon "Nuclear weapon"),[109] to secure Iran's [oil resources](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Oil_resources "Oil resources"),[110][111] and to [achieve regime change](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Regime_change_efforts_in_the_2026_Iran_war "Regime change efforts in the 2026 Iran war") by bringing the [Iranian opposition](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iranian_opposition "Iranian opposition") to power.[112][113][114][115] Iranian[116] and some US officials rejected claims that Iran had been preparing an attack.[117] The [International Atomic Energy Agency](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/International_Atomic_Energy_Agency "International Atomic Energy Agency") (IAEA) said that while Iran has an "ambitious" nuclear program and has refused to allow inspections of its damaged sites since the 2025 war, there was no evidence of a structured nuclear weapons program when the 2026 war began.[118] [UN](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/UN "UN") secretary-general [Antonio Guterres](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Antonio_Guterres "Antonio Guterres") and several uninvolved countries condemned the US–Israeli strikes; the [United Nations Security Council](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_Nations_Security_Council "United Nations Security Council") later passed [a resolution](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_Nations_Security_Council_Resolution_2817 "United Nations Security Council Resolution 2817") condemning Iran's retaliatory strikes on the Gulf states.[119] Critics of the war, including legal and international relations experts, have described the attacks as illegal under US law, an act of [imperialism](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/American_imperialism "American imperialism") and a violation of Iran's [sovereignty](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sovereignty "Sovereignty")[120][121] under [international law](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/International_law "International law").[122]
[The war's economic impact](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Economic_impact_of_the_2026_Iran_war "Economic impact of the 2026 Iran war"), described as the world's largest supply disruption since the [1970s energy crisis](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1970s_energy_crisis "1970s energy crisis"),[123] includes surges in [oil](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Price_of_oil "Price of oil") and [gas prices](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Natural_gas_prices "Natural gas prices"), wide [disruptions in aviation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Economic_impact_of_the_2026_Iran_war#Aviation "Economic impact of the 2026 Iran war") and tourism, and [volatility in financial markets](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Economic_impact_of_the_2026_Iran_war#Financial_markets "Economic impact of the 2026 Iran war"). Oil and gas shipments were disrupted when Iran [closed the Strait of Hormuz](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Strait_of_Hormuz_crisis "2026 Strait of Hormuz crisis") and Israel and Iran attacked energy facilities.[124][125][126] On 19 March, the cost of the war to the US military was estimated at US$18 billion,[127] and [the Pentagon](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Pentagon "The Pentagon") requested an additional US$200 billion for the war.[128][129]
## Background
Main articles: [Rationale for the 2026 Iran war](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rationale_for_the_2026_Iran_war "Rationale for the 2026 Iran war") and [Regime change efforts in the 2026 Iran war](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Regime_change_efforts_in_the_2026_Iran_war "Regime change efforts in the 2026 Iran war")
Further information: [Middle Eastern crisis (2023–present)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Middle_Eastern_crisis_\(2023%E2%80%93present\) "Middle Eastern crisis \(2023–present\)")
### Iran's relations with the United States and Israel
Further information: [Iran–United States relations](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iran%E2%80%93United_States_relations "Iran–United States relations") and [Iran–Israel relations](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iran%E2%80%93Israel_relations "Iran–Israel relations")
A US- and UK-backed [coup d'état in 1953](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1953_Iranian_coup_d%27%C3%A9tat "1953 Iranian coup d'état") deposed the democratically elected Iranian prime minister [Mohammad Mosaddegh](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mohammad_Mosaddegh "Mohammad Mosaddegh") due to his [nationalization of the oil industry](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nationalization_of_the_Iranian_oil_industry "Nationalization of the Iranian oil industry") and strengthened the rule of the [Shah](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shah "Shah"), [Mohammad Reza Pahlavi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mohammad_Reza_Pahlavi "Mohammad Reza Pahlavi").[130][131][132] Israel maintained close ties with Iran as part of their [alliance of the periphery](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alliance_of_the_periphery "Alliance of the periphery") strategy.[133] Resentment of the Shah's deference to Western interests and his autocratic rule led to the [1979 revolution](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1979_revolution "1979 revolution") in which he was overthrown,[130][134] after which Iran became an [Islamic republic](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Islamic_republic "Islamic republic"). The new regime, seeing both the United States and Israel (themselves close allies) as meddling in the region, and viewing Israel as an occupier of Palestinian land, severed diplomatic ties with both nations and held the [American embassy staff hostage](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iran_hostage_crisis "Iran hostage crisis") for over a year.[130][133]
During the [Iran–Iraq War](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iran%E2%80%93Iraq_War "Iran–Iraq War") in the 1980s, the [US provided economic, intelligence, and indirect military support to Iraq](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_States_support_for_Iraq_during_the_Iran%E2%80%93Iraq_War "United States support for Iraq during the Iran–Iraq War"). Toward the end of the war in 1988, a US warship was struck by an Iranian mine; in response, the US [attacked Iran's navy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Operation_Praying_Mantis "Operation Praying Mantis"). A few months later, the US mistakenly shot down a [civilian Iranian flight](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iran_Air_Flight_655 "Iran Air Flight 655").[135] Throughout this period, Iran started a ballistic missile program to deter Iraqi [missile attacks on Iranian cities](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/War_of_the_Cities "War of the Cities") and to compensate for its lack of modern air force due to sanctions.[136] Iran began to engage in [proxy conflicts](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Proxy_conflicts "Proxy conflicts") in the region, backing [Hezbollah](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hezbollah "Hezbollah") in Lebanon. In 2002, Iran's nuclear program was revealed, which was met with [economic sanctions](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/International_sanctions_against_Iran "International sanctions against Iran") and a [US–Israel cyberattack](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Operation_Olympic_Games "Operation Olympic Games") on Iran.[135] In the 2000s, Iran began supporting militias fighting the US in Iraq. These proxies became part of an informal [Axis of Resistance](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Axis_of_Resistance "Axis of Resistance") committed primarily to countering the influence of the US and Israel in the Middle East. The conflict between the US and Iran became direct in January 2020 when President Trump, during his first term, ordered the [assassination of Qasem Soleimani](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Assassination_of_Qasem_Soleimani "Assassination of Qasem Soleimani"), commander of the [Quds Force](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quds_Force "Quds Force").[130][137]
Tensions between Iran and the US and Israel further escalated following the [October 7 attacks](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/October_7_attacks "October 7 attacks") on Israel and the start of the [Gaza war](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gaza_war "Gaza war") in 2023, during which Israel weakened Iranian-backed militias across the Middle East, including [Hamas](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hamas "Hamas") in Gaza,[s] Hezbollah, and others. [Israeli strikes on the Iranian consulate in Damascus](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Israeli_airstrike_on_the_Iranian_consulate_in_Damascus "Israeli airstrike on the Iranian consulate in Damascus") and the [assassination of Ismail Haniyeh](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Assassination_of_Ismail_Haniyeh "Assassination of Ismail Haniyeh") in Iran were met with strikes on Israel in [April](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/April_2024_Iranian_strikes_on_Israel "April 2024 Iranian strikes on Israel") and [October](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/October_2024_Iranian_strikes_on_Israel "October 2024 Iranian strikes on Israel"). Israel waged the [Twelve-Day War](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Twelve-Day_War "Twelve-Day War") on Iran in June 2025, which included [an American airstrike](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_States_strikes_on_Iranian_nuclear_sites "United States strikes on Iranian nuclear sites") aimed at destroying Iran's nuclear facilities.[140]
### Iran nuclear issue
Further information: [Iran and weapons of mass destruction § Nuclear weapons](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iran_and_weapons_of_mass_destruction#Nuclear_weapons "Iran and weapons of mass destruction")
See also: [Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons § Iran](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Treaty_on_the_Non-Proliferation_of_Nuclear_Weapons#Iran "Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons")
Iran's [AMAD Project](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/AMAD_Project "AMAD Project") was suspended pursuant to [Ali Khamenei's fatwa against nuclear weapons](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ali_Khamenei%27s_fatwa_against_nuclear_weapons "Ali Khamenei's fatwa against nuclear weapons") in 2003. Khamenei, the [supreme leader of Iran](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Supreme_leader_of_Iran "Supreme leader of Iran") at the time and one of the world's most senior Shiite religious leaders, issued his religious ruling banning the development of nuclear weapons of any kind.[141][142] Iran has said it is not seeking nuclear weapons, and that its enrichment efforts are to generate [nuclear power](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nuclear_power "Nuclear power") for civilian use.[143] Some UK and US analysts concluded Iran is pursuing a strategy of [nuclear hedging](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nuclear_hedging "Nuclear hedging").[144] [UN Security Council](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/UN_Security_Council "UN Security Council") (UNSC) concerns about the [nuclear program of Iran](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nuclear_program_of_Iran "Nuclear program of Iran") from 2006[t] led to the creation of the [Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Joint_Comprehensive_Plan_of_Action "Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action") (JCPOA) in 2015.[148] The [US withdrew from the JCPOA](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_States_withdrawal_from_the_JCPOA "United States withdrawal from the JCPOA") in 2018[149][134] and re-imposed [sanctions on Iran](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_States_sanctions_against_Iran "United States sanctions against Iran"), as part of a US turn toward the use of force rather than diplomacy in its relations with Iran. The [first Trump administration](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/First_Trump_administration "First Trump administration") adopted a "[maximum pressure](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Maximum_pressure_campaign "Maximum pressure campaign")" strategy.[150] The [Biden administration](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Biden_administration "Biden administration") did not relax the economic sanctions against Iran, and instead implemented more measures.[151]
The [second Trump administration](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Second_Trump_administration "Second Trump administration") reimposed the "maximum pressure" approach on Iran.[152][134] [Secretary of the Treasury](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Secretary_of_the_Treasury "Secretary of the Treasury") [Scott Bessent](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Scott_Bessent "Scott Bessent") labelled the collapse of the Iranian currency in December 2025 as the "grand culmination" of this strategy.[153][154] At the [State of the Union Address](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_State_of_the_Union_Address "2026 State of the Union Address") on 24 February 2026, President Trump stated that Iran had restarted its nuclear program and was developing missiles capable of striking the US.[155] A few days later, the [International Atomic Energy Agency](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/International_Atomic_Energy_Agency "International Atomic Energy Agency") (IAEA) reported that Iran had stored [highly enriched uranium](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Highly_enriched_uranium "Highly enriched uranium") in an underground facility that was undamaged in the previous round of fighting.[156][157] The IAEA said that it had no evidence that Iran has an organized nuclear weapons program or is building an atomic bomb,[158][159][160] but could not be sure that Iran's broader nuclear program was "exclusively peaceful" at the time of reportage as the agency was denied access.[161]
### Israel nuclear issue
Further information: [Israel and nuclear weapons](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Israel_and_nuclear_weapons "Israel and nuclear weapons") and [Middle East nuclear weapon free zone](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Middle_East_nuclear_weapon_free_zone "Middle East nuclear weapon free zone")
Israel is [widely believed to possess nuclear weapons](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Israel_and_nuclear_weapons "Israel and nuclear weapons"), with a stockpile estimated between 90 and 400 nuclear warheads.[173] Israel is the only [nuclear-armed country](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_states_with_nuclear_weapons "List of states with nuclear weapons") that [does not officially acknowledge](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Israel_and_nuclear_weapons#Policy "Israel and nuclear weapons") its nuclear weapons capability.[174] Some scholars have argued that Israel's nuclear weapons have incentivized Iran to develop its own nuclear program, seen as maintaining a balance of [nuclear deterrence](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Deterrence_theory "Deterrence theory"), or causing a regional [nuclear arms race](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nuclear_arms_race "Nuclear arms race").[175][176][177][178] Citing security threats, Israel rejects international calls to accede to the [Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Treaty_on_the_Non-Proliferation_of_Nuclear_Weapons "Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons") or to participate in negotiations of a [Middle East nuclear weapon free zone](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Middle_East_nuclear_weapon_free_zone "Middle East nuclear weapon free zone").[179][180][181] This has frustrated both US[174] and UK[182] diplomatic efforts on such a zone. Scholars also suggest US tacit acceptance of the Israeli nuclear weapons capability is seen as a double standard that undermines its diplomatic credibility in Middle East [nonproliferation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nonproliferation "Nonproliferation") negotiations.[183][174]
## Prelude
Main article: [Prelude to the 2026 Iran war](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Prelude_to_the_2026_Iran_war "Prelude to the 2026 Iran war")
### Anti-government protests in Iran and initial US deployments
Further information: [2025–2026 Iranian protests](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2025%E2%80%932026_Iranian_protests "2025–2026 Iranian protests")
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/71/Iran_protests_8_January_2026.jpg/250px-Iran_protests_8_January_2026.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Iran_protests_8_January_2026.jpg)8 January: [Anti-government protests](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2025%E2%80%932026_Iranian_protests "2025–2026 Iranian protests") in [Tehran](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tehran "Tehran"). US president [Donald Trump](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Donald_Trump "Donald Trump") encouraged protests and threatened immediate military intervention if Iran killed peaceful protesters but held off for almost two months despite [massacres of protesters](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iran_massacres "2026 Iran massacres") as the [US built up its military presence in the region](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_United_States_military_buildup_in_the_Middle_East "2026 United States military buildup in the Middle East").
Beginning in late December 2025, massive [nationwide anti-government protests](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2025%E2%80%932026_Iranian_protests "2025–2026 Iranian protests") erupted in Iran. Originally driven by discontent over the [economic crisis](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iranian_economic_crisis "Iranian economic crisis"), they escalated into explicit calls for the overthrow of the [Islamic Republic government](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Government_of_the_Islamic_Republic_of_Iran "Government of the Islamic Republic of Iran"). The protests became the largest in scale since the 1979 revolution with 5 million Iranians protesting.[184][185] The Iranian government responded with [massacres of protesters](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iran_massacres "2026 Iran massacres"), with the deadliest incidents occurring on 8 to 10 January 2026.[186] The US-based [Human Rights Activists News Agency](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Human_Rights_Activists_News_Agency "Human Rights Activists News Agency") stated it had documented at least 7,007 deaths and was reviewing an additional 11,744,[187] the Iranian government said it was 3,117, while various non-government affiliated Iranian health officials gave a figure of 32,000 deaths.[188][189]
On 2 January 2026, Trump threatened a "locked and loaded" military intervention in Iran if the government decided to kill peaceful protesters.[190] On 13 January, he expressed support for Iranian anti-government protesters and pledged that "help is on the way" for them,[191] and later, on 23 January, Trump announced that a US "armada" was heading to the Middle East, including the aircraft carrier [USS  _Abraham Lincoln_](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/USS_Abraham_Lincoln_\(CVN-72\) "USS Abraham Lincoln \(CVN-72\)") and several guided-missile destroyers.[192][193] On 13 February, Trump ordered the aircraft carrier [USS  _Gerald R. Ford_](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/USS_Gerald_R._Ford "USS Gerald R. Ford") and its supporting warships to sail to the Middle East.[194]
### 2026 nuclear negotiations
Further information: [2025–2026 Iran–United States negotiations](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2025%E2%80%932026_Iran%E2%80%93United_States_negotiations "2025–2026 Iran–United States negotiations")
[](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:President_Trump_Gaggles_with_Press_Before_Departing_the_White_House,_Feb._27,_2026.webm "Play video")[](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:President_Trump_Gaggles_with_Press_Before_Departing_the_White_House,_Feb._27,_2026.webm)27 February: President Trump briefly discusses the United States' relationship with Iran with the press, roughly 24 hours before the war would begin.
On 6 February 2026, Iran and the US held indirect nuclear negotiations in [Oman](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Oman "Oman")'s capital, [Muscat](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Muscat "Muscat"). Iran emphasized that progress depends on consultations back in capitals.[195] A second round of nuclear talks was scheduled in Geneva.[195] Between 15 and 20 February, Iran increased its oil exports to three times the normal rate, and reduced its oil storage.[196]
Just before the strikes began, on 27 February 2026, Omani foreign minister [Badr Al-Busaidi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Badr_Al-Busaidi "Badr Al-Busaidi") said a "breakthrough" had been reached and Iran had agreed both to never stockpile enriched uranium and to full verification by the IAEA;[197] furthermore, Iran had agreed to irreversibly downgrade its current enriched uranium to "the lowest level possible". Al-Busaidi said peace was "within reach".[198][197] Talks were expected to resume on 2 March.[197] After the US and Israel attacked Iran, Al-Busaidi said that he was dismayed and that "active and serious negotiations" had been undermined.[199]
US Middle East envoy [Steve Witkoff](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Steve_Witkoff "Steve Witkoff") said Iran had begun recent nuclear talks by insisting on its "inalienable right" to [enrich uranium](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Enriched_uranium "Enriched uranium"), rejecting a US proposal for zero enrichment, and even boasting that its 460 kilograms of 60% enriched uranium could produce 11 nuclear bombs.[200][201] Diplomats with knowledge of the Iran talks said Witkoff undermined the negotiations by misrepresenting the key exchange, stating that the US negotiators misunderstood the Iranian proposal, including Iran's offer to suspend uranium enrichment for several years and why Iran did not trust the US offer of nuclear fuel supplies.[202]
### US military buildup
Further information: [2026 United States military buildup in the Middle East](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_United_States_military_buildup_in_the_Middle_East "2026 United States military buildup in the Middle East")
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/35/Abraham_Lincoln_Carrier_Strike_Group_Conducts_Photo_Exercise.jpg/250px-Abraham_Lincoln_Carrier_Strike_Group_Conducts_Photo_Exercise.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Abraham_Lincoln_Carrier_Strike_Group_Conducts_Photo_Exercise.jpg)6 February: [Carrier Strike Group 3](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Carrier_Strike_Group_3 "Carrier Strike Group 3") sails in formation in the [Arabian Sea](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Arabian_Sea "Arabian Sea") during the US military buildup
On 14 February, the US publicly began preparing its campaign.[203] By 19 February the [US buildup](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_United_States_military_buildup_in_the_Middle_East "2026 United States military buildup in the Middle East") was described as the largest in the Middle East since the [2003 invasion of Iraq](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2003_invasion_of_Iraq "2003 invasion of Iraq").[204] Netanyahu called Trump on 23 February to inform him about Khamenei's upcoming meeting with his top advisors and its location.[205][206] On 24 February, during [the State of the Union Address](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_State_of_the_Union_Address "2026 State of the Union Address"), Trump accused Iran of reviving efforts to build nuclear weapons and advanced missile capabilities that could threaten the US, Europe, and US bases overseas. He warned that the US was prepared to act if necessary.[207] American intelligence reports suggested that alleged threats of long-range Iranian ballistic missiles were unfounded, with such capabilities requiring up until 2035 should Iran have decided to pursue the project.[208]
On 25 February 2026, Iranian foreign minister [Abbas Araghchi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Abbas_Araghchi "Abbas Araghchi") stated that a "historic" agreement with the United States to avert military conflict was "within reach" ahead of renewed talks in Geneva. Araghchi emphasized that diplomacy must be prioritized to avoid further escalation. Despite high tensions and a significant US military buildup in the region, Araghchi reiterated on social media that Iran holds a "crystal clear" position against developing nuclear weapons, while defending its right to peaceful nuclear technology.[209]
According to _[The Wall Street Journal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Wall_Street_Journal "The Wall Street Journal")_ , US senator [Lindsey Graham](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lindsey_Graham "Lindsey Graham") made the most compelling case to Trump for an assault on Iran.[210] According to _[The Washington Post](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Washington_Post "The Washington Post")_ , crown prince of Saudi Arabia [Mohammed bin Salman](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mohammed_bin_Salman "Mohammed bin Salman") had multiple phone calls with Trump urging him to attack Iran,[211] and Trump's decision to attack Iran came after the [Saudi Arabian](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saudi_Arabia_lobby_in_the_United_States "Saudi Arabia lobby in the United States") and [Israeli](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Israel_lobby_in_the_United_States "Israel lobby in the United States") governments lobbied him repeatedly to make the move.[212] The claim was denied by Saudi Arabia.[213]
[![Benjamin Netanyahu \(right\) shakes hands with American politician Marco Rubio \(left\).](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/8b/Marco_Rubio_visits_Israel_from_February_15_to_17%2C_2025_-_18.jpg/120px-Marco_Rubio_visits_Israel_from_February_15_to_17%2C_2025_-_18.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Marco_Rubio_visits_Israel_from_February_15_to_17,_2025_-_18.jpg)
Israeli prime minister [Benjamin Netanyahu](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Benjamin_Netanyahu "Benjamin Netanyahu") successfully lobbied the Trump administration for military intervention.
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/37/Donald_Trump_state_visit_to_Saudi_Arabia%2C_2025-05-13_P20250513DT-1367_%28cropped%29.jpg/120px-Donald_Trump_state_visit_to_Saudi_Arabia%2C_2025-05-13_P20250513DT-1367_%28cropped%29.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Donald_Trump_state_visit_to_Saudi_Arabia,_2025-05-13_P20250513DT-1367_\(cropped\).jpg)
Saudi crown prince [Mohammed bin Salman](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mohammed_bin_Salman "Mohammed bin Salman") reportedly urged Trump to strike Iran despite public calls for diplomacy.
_[The Guardian](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Guardian "The Guardian")_ reported a few days before the attack that the decision would be determined by the outcome of a meeting in [Geneva](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Geneva "Geneva"), in which the US would be led by Steve Witkoff and [Jared Kushner](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jared_Kushner "Jared Kushner").[214] On the morning of the attack, some of Iran's top military and intelligence leaders, including Khamenei, were gathered together in the national security council offices for meetings.[215] US and Israeli military intelligence officers identified that the senior Iranian leaders would gather at three meetings that could be struck simultaneously.[216] Khamenei and his officials were above ground and in broad daylight. It is unknown how US and Israeli leadership were aware of their exact location.[217]
A few days before the attack, senior advisors for the Trump administration said it would be better if Israel strikes Iran first, so that the United States would have a better justification for going to war after Iran retaliates.[218] US secretary of state [Marco Rubio](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Marco_Rubio "Marco Rubio") later disclosed that the initial US attack on Iran was due to an Israeli intention to attack Iranian leadership, which would have jeopardized US forces in the region.[219] He also stated that the United States may have followed Israel into the conflict;[220] though Trump disagreed.[221]
