[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/3e/President_Donald_J._Trump_oversees_Operation_Epic_Fury_at_Mar-a-Lago%2C_Palm_Beach%2C_FL%2C_Feb._28%2C_2026._%28White_House_photo_by_Daniel_Torok%29_%2855121599389%29.jpg/250px-President_Donald_J._Trump_oversees_Operation_Epic_Fury_at_Mar-a-Lago%2C_Palm_Beach%2C_FL%2C_Feb._28%2C_2026._%28White_House_photo_by_Daniel_Torok%29_%2855121599389%29.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:President_Donald_J._Trump_oversees_Operation_Epic_Fury_at_Mar-a-Lago,_Palm_Beach,_FL,_Feb._28,_2026._\(White_House_photo_by_Daniel_Torok\)_\(55121599389\).jpg)28 February: Trump and members of his cabinet oversee the war on Iran from the [Mar-a-Lago](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mar-a-Lago "Mar-a-Lago") resort in [Palm Beach, Florida](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Palm_Beach,_Florida "Palm Beach, Florida").[u] [![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/ef/%D7%94%D7%AA%D7%A8%D7%90%D7%94_%D7%91%D7%90%D7%A4%D7%9C%D7%99%D7%A7%D7%A6%D7%99%D7%94_%D7%A4%D7%99%D7%A7%D7%95%D7%93_%D7%94%D7%A2%D7%95%D7%A8%D7%A3_%D7%A2%D7%9C_%D7%A1%D7%9B%D7%A0%D7%AA_%D7%99%D7%A8%D7%99_%D7%98%D7%99%D7%9C%D7%99%D7%9D_%D7%91%D7%A0%D7%A9%D7%A8_%D7%91-28-2-26.jpeg/250px-%D7%94%D7%AA%D7%A8%D7%90%D7%94_%D7%91%D7%90%D7%A4%D7%9C%D7%99%D7%A7%D7%A6%D7%99%D7%94_%D7%A4%D7%99%D7%A7%D7%95%D7%93_%D7%94%D7%A2%D7%95%D7%A8%D7%A3_%D7%A2%D7%9C_%D7%A1%D7%9B%D7%A0%D7%AA_%D7%99%D7%A8%D7%99_%D7%98%D7%99%D7%9C%D7%99%D7%9D_%D7%91%D7%A0%D7%A9%D7%A8_%D7%91-28-2-26.jpeg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:%D7%94%D7%AA%D7%A8%D7%90%D7%94_%D7%91%D7%90%D7%A4%D7%9C%D7%99%D7%A7%D7%A6%D7%99%D7%94_%D7%A4%D7%99%D7%A7%D7%95%D7%93_%D7%94%D7%A2%D7%95%D7%A8%D7%A3_%D7%A2%D7%9C_%D7%A1%D7%9B%D7%A0%D7%AA_%D7%99%D7%A8%D7%99_%D7%98%D7%99%D7%9C%D7%99%D7%9D_%D7%91%D7%A0%D7%A9%D7%A8_%D7%91-28-2-26.jpeg)Warning in the [Home Front Command](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Home_Front_Command "Home Front Command") app in Israel on the first day of the war
On 27 February at 3:38 p.m. [EST](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eastern_Time_Zone#Eastern_Standard_Time "Eastern Time Zone") (11:08 p.m. [IRST](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iran_Standard_Time "Iran Standard Time")), Donald Trump, traveling on [Air Force One](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Air_Force_One "Air Force One") to [Corpus Christi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Corpus_Christi,_Texas "Corpus Christi, Texas"), Texas, gave the order to proceed with **Operation Epic Fury**.[222][215] US missiles, drones, and [Israeli fighter jets](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Israeli_Air_Force#Current_inventory "Israeli Air Force") began striking Iran the next day, around 9:45 a.m. IRST (1:15 a.m. EST).[223][224] The strikes took place during active negotiations over Iran's nuclear program.[225][226] The attack coincided with the beginning of the holy month of [Ramadan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ramadan "Ramadan"), when Muslim people practice [fasting](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Islamic_fasting "Islamic fasting") between dawn and sunset.[227]
The operation was codenamed **Operation Roaring Lion** [v] by Israel.[228] The usage of the word lion refers to both [pre-revolutionary Iran](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lion_and_Sun_flag "Lion and Sun flag") and the Jewish people.[229] The [Israeli Air Force](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Israeli_Air_Force "Israeli Air Force") (IAF) said that it had struck 500 military targets in western and central Iran, including air defenses and missile launchers, using about 200 fighter jets, in the largest combat sortie in its history.[230] Iranian naval vessels were also targeted.[231] Israel later said its initial strikes used over 1,200 bombs in 24 hours.[232] A US official said dozens of US strikes were carried out by planes based around the Middle East and from one or more aircraft carriers.[233]
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/4b/U_S_Forces_Launch_Operation_Epic_Fury_%289542157%29.jpg/250px-U_S_Forces_Launch_Operation_Epic_Fury_%289542157%29.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:U_S_Forces_Launch_Operation_Epic_Fury_\(9542157\).jpg)27 February: US aircraft sit on the flight deck of [USS  _Abraham Lincoln_](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/USS_Abraham_Lincoln_\(CVN-72\) "USS Abraham Lincoln \(CVN-72\)")
According to Iran International, quoting the [Iranian Students' News Agency](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iranian_Students%27_News_Agency "Iranian Students' News Agency"), thousands of [Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Islamic_Revolutionary_Guard_Corps "Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps") (IRGC) personnel, including several senior officials,[234] were killed or wounded[ _[vague](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Vagueness "Wikipedia:Vagueness")_ ] as several military bases were attacked.[235] Several strikes hit Tehran's [Pasteur Street](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pasteur_Street "Pasteur Street") district, where the presidential palace is, and the [National Security Council](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Supreme_National_Security_Council "Supreme National Security Council").[236][237]
The strikes were coupled with cyberattacks on Iranian infrastructure, media, and phone apps, including the BadeSaba Calendar prayer app, with messages calling on Iranians to rise up against their government.[238][239][240] The cyberattacks resulted in a near-total internet blackout in Iran, lasting over 60 hours with connectivity dropping to as low as 1% of normal levels, disrupting government communications, state media, and public services.[241][239] Iran has since restricted internet access to its people, allowing access only for government-approved users.[242][243]
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/6f/Vice_President_JD_Vance_meets_with_members_of_the_Cabinet_in_the_Situation_Room_at_the_White_House_%2855127462186%29.jpg/250px-Vice_President_JD_Vance_meets_with_members_of_the_Cabinet_in_the_Situation_Room_at_the_White_House_%2855127462186%29.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Vice_President_JD_Vance_meets_with_members_of_the_Cabinet_in_the_Situation_Room_at_the_White_House_\(55127462186\).jpg)28 February: Vice President [JD Vance](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/JD_Vance "JD Vance") with cabinet members in the [Situation Room](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Situation_Room "Situation Room") during the strikes
Israeli military officials said months-long planning preceded their strikes, allowing them to pinpoint their targets, attain "tactical surprise", and gain US support.[244] Attacking during the morning, rather than at night which was when most of Israel's previous attacks on Iran took place, added to the element of surprise.[217] After the strikes began, Israel declared a state of emergency, citing expectation of an Iranian retaliation, and warned its citizens to remain in protected areas.[245][246] Israel called up 20,000 reservists in addition to the 50,000 already on duty.[247] The [Israeli Ministry of Health](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Israeli_Ministry_of_Health "Israeli Ministry of Health") moved its hospital operations underground.[248] Israel warned Iranian civilians residing near military industries and infrastructure to evacuate immediately.[235]
[](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:President_Donald_J._Trump_on_the_United_States_Military_Major_Combat_Operations_in_Iran.webm "Play video")[](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:President_Donald_J._Trump_on_the_United_States_Military_Major_Combat_Operations_in_Iran.webm)28 February: Trump addresses the nation about the strikes.
At 2:30 a.m. EST on 28 February, Donald Trump released an eight-minute video statement on [Truth Social](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Truth_Social "Truth Social"), saying that the purpose of the US strikes in Iran was effectively[ _[vague](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Vagueness "Wikipedia:Vagueness")_ ] [regime change](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Regime_change "Regime change"). Trump said that Iran's "menacing activities" endangered the US and its allies. He cited the [Iran hostage crisis](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iran_hostage_crisis "Iran hostage crisis"), support for proxies such as [Hamas](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hamas "Hamas") and [Hezbollah](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hezbollah "Hezbollah"), and [its killings of protesters](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Human_rights_in_Iran "Human rights in Iran").[249] Trump cited Iran calling "[Death to America](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Death_to_America "Death to America")" and its history of attacking US civilians and soldiers as evidence of Iran's intent against the United States.[109][250] Trump and [Benjamin Netanyahu](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Benjamin_Netanyahu "Benjamin Netanyahu") both warned of the potential threat of nuclear weapons in Iran and called on the Iranian people to overthrow their government.[249][251] _[The Times of Israel](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Times_of_Israel "The Times of Israel")_ reported that Defense Minister [Israel Katz](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Israel_Katz "Israel Katz") labeled the strikes a "pre-emptive attack" intended to "remove threats to the State of Israel".[245] Iran rejected claims that it intended to attack the United States, citing the aggressive posture of the US armed forces as evidence.[116]
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/8e/US_bases_in_the_Middle_East.svg/250px-US_bases_in_the_Middle_East.svg.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:US_bases_in_the_Middle_East.svg)Major [US military bases and installations](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_American_military_installations "List of American military installations") in the Middle East, including bases used during the strikes
Iranian forces reacted within hours by launching missiles and drones. Iran codenamed its military response **Operation True Promise IV**.[w][x] Iranian state media also dubbed the events as the **Ramadan War** ,[y] a term similar to the one used by Egypt and Syria during the [Yom Kippur War](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yom_Kippur_War "Yom Kippur War") of 1973 against Israel.[252]
Iran struck Israeli targets in [Tel Aviv](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tel_Aviv "Tel Aviv") and [Haifa](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Haifa "Haifa")[253] as well as multiple countries throughout the Persian Gulf region.[254] Major targets included Bahrain's capital [Manama](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Manama "Manama"),[255][256] [Kuwait International Airport](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kuwait_International_Airport "Kuwait International Airport"), the United Arab Emirates' capital [Abu Dhabi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Abu_Dhabi "Abu Dhabi"),[257][258] [Riyadh](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Riyadh "Riyadh") and [Eastern Province](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eastern_Province,_Saudi_Arabia "Eastern Province, Saudi Arabia") in Saudi Arabia,[259] and [Erbil International Airport](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Erbil_International_Airport "Erbil International Airport") and the [US Consulate General in Erbil](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Consulate_General_of_the_United_States_in_Erbil "Consulate General of the United States in Erbil") in the Kurdistan Region of Iraq.[260]
Iran reported striking the US bases of [Al Udeid Air Base](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Al_Udeid_Air_Base "Al Udeid Air Base") in Qatar, [Ali Al Salem Air Base](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ali_Al_Salem_Air_Base "Ali Al Salem Air Base") in Kuwait,[261] [Al Dhafra Air Base](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Al_Dhafra_Air_Base "Al Dhafra Air Base") in the UAE, and the US Navy [Fifth Fleet](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fifth_Fleet "Fifth Fleet") headquarters in Bahrain.[262] Local sources said Iran used [Shahed drones](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shahed_drones "Shahed drones"), which are among the weapons most used by Iran and its proxies, including Hamas and Hezbollah.[263] On 1 or 2 March, Iran struck the radar bases for US [THAAD](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Terminal_High_Altitude_Area_Defense "Terminal High Altitude Area Defense") missile defense systems in Jordan, the UAE and Saudi Arabia, destroying at least one of them.[264]
[](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Operation_Epic_Fury_Update,_President_Donald_J._Trump.webm "Play video")[](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Operation_Epic_Fury_Update,_President_Donald_J._Trump.webm)1 March: President Trump delivers an update on the strikes, including the assassination of Ali Khamenei.
Iran's top security official, [Ali Larijani](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ali_Larijani "Ali Larijani"), announced the [Interim Leadership Council](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Interim_Leadership_Council "Interim Leadership Council") on 1 March and accused the US and Israel of trying to dismantle Iran. He warned "secessionist groups" of severe consequences if they take action.[265] Trump relayed to [NBC News](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/NBC_News "NBC News") that "a large amount of leadership" in Iran had been killed.[266] Israel launched a new wave of strikes against Iranian targets.[265] Iran launched missiles and drones on Bahrain, Israel, Jordan, Kuwait, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, and the UAE.[267][ _[better  source needed](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability#Questionable_sources "Wikipedia:Verifiability")_][268][ _[better  source needed](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability#Questionable_sources "Wikipedia:Verifiability")_][269] The United Kingdom initially reported there were strikes against Cyprus,[270][271] but later confirmed that there were not.[272] Two oil tankers, the [Palau](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Palau "Palau")-flagged _Skylight_ and the [Marshall Islands](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Marshall_Islands "Marshall Islands")-flagged _MKD VYOM_ , were targeted off the coast of Oman.[273][274][275] Shipping through the Strait of Hormuz slowed to a standstill,[276] with 150 freight ships, including many oil tankers, stalled.[277]
To avert further Iranian strikes, the [E3](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/EU_three#E3 "EU three") (the UK, France, and Germany) resolved to back, if needed, "proportionate military defensive measures" against drones and ballistic missiles, signaling the potential for direct involvement.[278] British prime minister [Keir Starmer](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Keir_Starmer "Keir Starmer") said that US can use British bases for "defensive" strikes on Iran[279] and disclosed that Ukrainian and other specialists would aid Gulf efforts to foil Iranian drone strikes.[280] [CNN](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CNN "CNN") reported that Saudi Crown Prince Salman, with US backing, vowed to employ military force against further Iranian incursions, calling Iranian strikes "cowardly" due to Saudi airspace being closed to US and Israeli attackers.[281] On 1 March, Trump announced that the US had accepted an Iranian proposal to further negotiations.[282] Trump later said the US operations were to be completed within a four-week timetable in an interview with the _[Daily Mail](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Daily_Mail "Daily Mail")_.[283] However, Ali Larijani subsequently ruled out talks.[284]
[](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Epic_Fury_Strike_compilation_\(997855\).webm "Play video")[](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Epic_Fury_Strike_compilation_\(997855\).webm)2 March: Montage of [US Central Command](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/U.S._Central_Command "U.S. Central Command") strikes
On 2 March, the [US embassy in Kuwait](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/US_embassy_in_Kuwait "US embassy in Kuwait") was struck and subsequently closed indefinitely; no casualties were reported.[285][286] The US-flagged tanker _Stena Imperative_ and the Honduras-flagged tanker _Athe Nova_ were also struck.[287] A senior IRGC official who is an advisor to the IRGC commander said he would set fire onto any ship coming through the Strait of Hormuz, and added that no oil will leave the area.[288] Qatar shot down two [Iranian Su-24 bombers](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sukhoi_Su-24 "Sukhoi Su-24"), making it the first nation to shoot down an Iranian aircraft in the conflict.[289] The Qatari Ministry of Defense announced that [Ras Laffan Industrial City](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ras_Laffan_Industrial_City "Ras Laffan Industrial City") and [Mesaieed Industrial Area](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mesaieed_Industrial_Area "Mesaieed Industrial Area") were struck by two Iranian drones.[290] On 6 March, it was reported that according to satellite imagery analysis by both _[Bloomberg](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bloomberg_News "Bloomberg News")_ and the Energy Economics and Society Research Institute in Tokyo, Ras Laffan, the main gas facility in Qatar, appeared to have not been damaged before the "unprecedented shutdown" which sent fuel prices higher.[291]
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/35/Attack_on_Sanandaj_Residential_Areas_1.jpg/250px-Attack_on_Sanandaj_Residential_Areas_1.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Attack_on_Sanandaj_Residential_Areas_1.jpg)2 March: Damage to residential buildings in [Sanandaj](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sanandaj "Sanandaj"), Iranian Kurdistan
The United States and Israel attacked the [Natanz Nuclear Facility](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Natanz_Nuclear_Facility "Natanz Nuclear Facility")[292] and the Iranian media reported damage to the Khatam-al-Anbia and Gandhi hospitals.[293] A [friendly fire](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Friendly_fire "Friendly fire") incident took place when the pilot of an [F/A-18](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/F/A-18 "F/A-18") of the [Kuwait Air Force](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kuwait_Air_Force "Kuwait Air Force") shot down three US [F-15E](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/F-15E "F-15E") fighters.[294][295][296][286] Israel and Hezbollah exchanged strikes against one another.[297] Hezbollah began firing rockets and missiles on Israel in retaliation for the killing of Khamenei.[298][299] Hezbollah later claimed that the attack was a "defensive act" after over a year of Israeli [attacks](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hezbollah%E2%80%93Israel_conflict_\(2023%E2%80%93present\) "Hezbollah–Israel conflict \(2023–present\)") despite a [truce](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2024_Israel%E2%80%93Lebanon_ceasefire_agreement "2024 Israel–Lebanon ceasefire agreement").[300] It added that it restarted fighting to force Israel to stop its aggression and evacuate from [seized Lebanese territories](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Israeli%E2%80%93Lebanese_conflict "Israeli–Lebanese conflict"), emphasizing that the move was unrelated to the war.[301] Lebanon banned military activities by Hezbollah after the attacks.[302] An IDF spokesperson issued an emergency statement stating that the attack is to be considered "an official declaration of war by Hezbollah", vowing to "neutralize" the threat.[297] Israel struck [southern Lebanon](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Southern_Lebanon "Southern Lebanon"),[303][77][304] Beirut, and the [Beqaa Valley](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Beqaa_Valley "Beqaa Valley").[297] The IDF said that it killed the head of Hezbollah's intelligence headquarters [Hussein Makled](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Hussein_Makled&action=edit&redlink=1 "Hussein Makled \(page does not exist\)") in the strikes.[305][306] In Lebanon, the IDF killed Adham Adnan al-Othman, the head of Palestinian Islamic Jihad's armed wing, [Al-Quds Brigades](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Al-Quds_Brigades "Al-Quds Brigades"), in Lebanon.[307]
The Royal Air Force station at [Akrotiri](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/RAF_Akrotiri "RAF Akrotiri"), Cyprus, was targeted by a drone strike around midnight local time, with one causing minor damage.[308][309][310][311] Another strike on Cyprus led to [Greece](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Greece "Greece") announcing that they would deploy frigates and [F-16s](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/F-16 "F-16") to defend Cyprus from any further strikes by Iran.[312] Later in the day IRGC general [Sardar Jabbari](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Sardar_Jabbari&action=edit&redlink=1 "Sardar Jabbari \(page does not exist\)") commented that there was a US presence on the island of Cyprus and that Iran planned to strike the island "with such intensity that the Americans will be forced to leave".[313][314] The pro-Iran militant group [Guardians of the Blood Brigade](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iraqi_Guardians_of_Blood_Brigades "Iraqi Guardians of Blood Brigades") claimed responsibility for attacks on the US [Victory Base](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Victory_Base_Complex "Victory Base Complex") near [Baghdad International Airport](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Baghdad_International_Airport "Baghdad International Airport") and [Erbil](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Erbil "Erbil"), [Kurdistan Region](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kurdistan_Region "Kurdistan Region"), Iraq.[77][315][316] The [Islamic Resistance in Iraq](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Islamic_Resistance_in_Iraq "Islamic Resistance in Iraq") claimed responsibility for over 23 drone strikes on US assets in Erbil.[317]
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c5/Attack_around_Enghelab_Square_1.jpg/250px-Attack_around_Enghelab_Square_1.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Attack_around_Enghelab_Square_1.jpg)3 March: [Enghelab Square](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Enghelab_Square "Enghelab Square"), Tehran
On 3 March, US and Israeli strikes reportedly destroyed the [Supreme National Security Council](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Supreme_National_Security_Council "Supreme National Security Council") (SNSC) headquarters,[318] the [Expediency Discernment Council](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Expediency_Discernment_Council "Expediency Discernment Council") building in Tehran,[319] and what Israeli officials described as an underground nuclear weapons facility called Min Zadai.[320] The proximity of these strikes to the [Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bushehr_Nuclear_Power_Plant "Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant") (about 12 km (7.5 mi) away) prompted the Russian agency [Rosatom](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rosatom "Rosatom") to suspend construction on new units and evacuate non-essential staff due to a loss of communication with Iranian officials.[321] US officials said US forces severely damaged Iran's naval capabilities, mainly in the Gulf of Oman, where several Iranian warships have reportedly been destroyed and key bases hit.[322][323] Debris from an airstrike damaged [Golestan Palace](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Golestan_Palace "Golestan Palace"), a [UNESCO World Heritage Site](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/UNESCO_World_Heritage_Site "UNESCO World Heritage Site"),[324] causing UNESCO to issue a statement that damaging UNESCO property is against international law.[325]
Israel Katz [authorized a ground invasion of Lebanon](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Lebanon_war "2026 Lebanon war") on 3 March.[326][327] They were later followed by a ground incursion in southern Lebanon with forces from the [91st Division](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/91st_Division_\(Israel\) "91st Division \(Israel\)") with the goal to establish a "security layer" for the Israeli residents of northern settlements against Hezbollah, expecting to destroy their infrastructure in the [Israel-Lebanon border](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Israel-Lebanon_border "Israel-Lebanon border"). The [Lebanese government](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lebanese_government "Lebanese government") reported that such Israeli [preventive attacks](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Preventive_attack "Preventive attack") reached [Kfarkela](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kfarkela "Kfarkela") and [Qouzah](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Al-Qaouzah "Al-Qaouzah"), provoking the [Lebanese army](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lebanese_army "Lebanese army") to do a redeployment from newly established border posts amid escalating Israeli activity in violation of the [2024 Israel–Lebanon ceasefire agreement](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2024_Israel%E2%80%93Lebanon_ceasefire_agreement "2024 Israel–Lebanon ceasefire agreement").[328] Israel also reported that it killed [Daoud Alizadeh](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Daoud_Alizadeh&action=edit&redlink=1 "Daoud Alizadeh \(page does not exist\)"), the commander of the Quds Force's Lebanon branch, in Tehran.[329]
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/8a/Attack_on_residential_homes_in_central_Tehran_1.jpg/250px-Attack_on_residential_homes_in_central_Tehran_1.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Attack_on_residential_homes_in_central_Tehran_1.jpg)4 March: Buildings in Tehran damaged by US-Israeli strikes
Western diplomats and other sources told _[The Jerusalem Post](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Jerusalem_Post "The Jerusalem Post")_ that Qatar had struck Iran after Iran had attempted to strike Doha's airport and Qatar shot down two Iranian [Su-24](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Su-24 "Su-24") bombers;[330] [Channel 12](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Channel_12_\(Israel\) "Channel 12 \(Israel\)") also reported Qatari strikes.[331] Qatari Foreign Ministry spokesperson [Majed al-Ansari](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Majed_al-Ansari "Majed al-Ansari") denied the accusation that Qatar had joined the "campaign targeting Iran".[332] An IRGC general warned it would hit all economic hubs in the Middle East,[333][334] and Abbas Araghchi said that any defensive European military involvement would be considered an act of war.[335][336]
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/97/Operation-Roaring-Lion-0011.jpg/250px-Operation-Roaring-Lion-0011.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Operation-Roaring-Lion-0011.jpg)An Israeli Air Force [F-35I](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/F-35I "F-35I") "Adir" shot down an Iranian [Yak-130](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yak-130 "Yak-130"), the first kill of a crewed aircraft by an [F-35](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/F-35 "F-35").
On 4 March, US secretary of state Marco Rubio announced that the US-Israeli attacks on Iran would increase in their intensity.[337] Israel attacks hit the [Basij](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Basij "Basij") headquarters.[338] [Mojtaba Khamenei](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mojtaba_Khamenei "Mojtaba Khamenei") survived an airstrike.[339] The IDF announced that an [F-35I](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/F-35I "F-35I") "Adir" shot down a Russian-made Iranian [Yak-130](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yak-130 "Yak-130") fighter jet over Tehran, the first time that an F-35 has ever shot down a crewed fighter jet in air-to-air combat and the first time the Israeli Air Force shot down an aircraft since 1985.[340]
Iran launched strikes against the [Al Udeid Air Base](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Al_Udeid_Air_Base "Al Udeid Air Base"),[341] as well as [Aramco's Ras Tanura](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Aramco_refinery_attack "2026 Aramco refinery attack") oil refining facility.[342] The United States ordered the evacuation of non-essential workers in Cyprus in anticipation of Iranian strikes and issued a travel warning for the country.[343][344] A ballistic missile launched from Iranian territory was intercepted by [NATO](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/NATO "NATO") integrated air defense systems as it entered Turkish airspace, marking a significant escalation in the regional conflict.[345] Turkey asserted its right to self-defense after the missile landed in [Dörtyol](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/D%C3%B6rtyol "Dörtyol"), [Hatay Province](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hatay_Province "Hatay Province"),[346] and NATO secretary general [Mark Rutte](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mark_Rutte "Mark Rutte") stated that the alliance was committed to defending Turkey.[347]
[](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Indian_Ocean_Iranian_vessel_\(998184\).webm "Play video")[](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Indian_Ocean_Iranian_vessel_\(998184\).webm)4 March: [USS  _Charlotte_](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/USS_Charlotte_\(SSN-766\) "USS Charlotte \(SSN-766\)") [torpedoed](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sinking_of_IRIS_Dena "Sinking of IRIS Dena") [IRIS  _Dena_](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IRIS_Dena "IRIS Dena")
An Iranian Navy frigate, [IRIS  _Dena_](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IRIS_Dena "IRIS Dena"), [was sunk](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sinking_of_IRIS_Dena "Sinking of IRIS Dena") in the Indian Ocean by [United States Navy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_States_Navy "United States Navy") submarine [USS  _Charlotte_](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/USS_Charlotte_\(SSN-766\) "USS Charlotte \(SSN-766\)"), about 40 nautical miles (74 km; 46 mi) south of [Galle](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Galle "Galle"), Sri Lanka. The vessel was returning to Iran following its participation in the [International Fleet Review 2026](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/International_Fleet_Review_2026 "International Fleet Review 2026") and the multilateral [Exercise MILAN](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Milan_\(naval_exercise\) "Milan \(naval exercise\)") at [Visakhapatnam](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Visakhapatnam "Visakhapatnam"), India.[348][349][350] The exercise required participating ships not to carry any ammunition, and the US likely knew the _Dena_ was defenseless, since it also sent a maritime patrol aircraft to participate.[351] It was the first ship sunk by a submarine in active combat since the [Falklands War](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Falklands_War "Falklands War"), and the first by an American submarine since [World War II](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/World_War_II "World War II").[z][354] According to the Iranian army, 104 Iranians were killed and 32 were injured from this attack.[355]
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/88/Armada_Espa%C3%B1ola_F105_Crist%C3%B3bal_Col%C3%B3n.jpg/250px-Armada_Espa%C3%B1ola_F105_Crist%C3%B3bal_Col%C3%B3n.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Armada_Espa%C3%B1ola_F105_Crist%C3%B3bal_Col%C3%B3n.jpg)The frigate [_Cristóbal Colón_](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Spanish_frigate_Crist%C3%B3bal_Col%C3%B3n "Spanish frigate Cristóbal Colón"), sent by Spain to protect Cyprus from aerial threats
The government of Azerbaijan said on 5 March that [two drones from Iran struck](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iranian_strikes_on_Azerbaijan "2026 Iranian strikes on Azerbaijan") Azerbaijan's [Nakhchivan exclave](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nakhchivan_exclave "Nakhchivan exclave")[356] damaging an airport and injuring two civilians.[357] Alarms went off in the UK's military bases on Cyprus twice during the visit of UK defence minister [John Healey](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/John_Healey "John Healey"), causing widespread panic and self-evacuations in the local population, but the bases confirmed in both instances that the missiles were not headed toward Cyprus.[358] Keir Starmer confirmed that the UK's bases on Cyprus would be used to defend the airspace of Jordan from Iranian drones,[359][360] while Italy, the Netherlands and Spain confirmed that they would be sending warships to defend Cyprus.[361][362][363] Ireland stated that they would be willing to protect Cyprus and join the European defense coalition that had mobilized around the island if requested.[364] The IDF killed Hamas official Wassim Attallah al-Ali in [Beddawi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Beddawi "Beddawi"), Lebanon.[365]
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/5e/Airstrike_on_the_residential_town_of_Shahid_Boroujerdi_20_Avash.webp/250px-Airstrike_on_the_residential_town_of_Shahid_Boroujerdi_20_Avash.webp.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Airstrike_on_the_residential_town_of_Shahid_Boroujerdi_20_Avash.webp)6 March: The aftermath of US-Israeli airstrikes in Tehran
Trump said on 6 March that there are "no time limits" for how long the war will continue for,[366] and Hegseth stated that the war has "only just begun".[367] Three Ghanaian peacekeepers enrolled in [UNIFIL](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/UNIFIL "UNIFIL") were hit by a missile and wounded in Southern Lebanon.[368] The Iranian tanker [IRIS  _Bushehr_](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IRIS_Bushehr "IRIS Bushehr") was interned by the [Sri Lanka Navy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sri_Lanka_Navy "Sri Lanka Navy"), marking the first instance of a warship being interned in a neutral country since World War II.[369]
### Second week (7–13 March)
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/88/P20260307DT-1619_The_Dignified_Transfer_of_Remains_of_Six_U.S._Soldiers.jpg/250px-P20260307DT-1619_The_Dignified_Transfer_of_Remains_of_Six_U.S._Soldiers.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:P20260307DT-1619_The_Dignified_Transfer_of_Remains_of_Six_U.S._Soldiers.jpg)Six dead US soldiers from the [2026 Port Shuaiba drone attack](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Port_Shuaiba_drone_attack "2026 Port Shuaiba drone attack") are transferred to the US on 7 March 2026.
On 7 March, the US sent a third aircraft carrier, [USS  _George H. W. Bush_](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/USS_George_H._W._Bush "USS George H. W. Bush"), to the Middle East.[370][371][372] The British aircraft carrier [HMS _Prince of Wales_](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/HMS_Prince_of_Wales_\(R09\) "HMS Prince of Wales \(R09\)") was placed on advanced readiness to defend British interests in the region.[373] Pezeskhian told reporters on 7 March that Iran would stop striking neighbors from 7 March onwards unless an attack originated from there.[374] Despite this, [Dubai International Airport](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dubai_International_Airport "Dubai International Airport") was later attacked by Iranian drones.[375] [NDTV](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/NDTV "NDTV") reported that following a week's hostilities, Iran might only have around one thousand ballistic missiles left.[376] On 7 March, ballistic missiles were fired from Bahrain toward Iran, likely by US forces.[377]
On 8 March, Khamenei's second son, [Mojtaba](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mojtaba_Khamenei "Mojtaba Khamenei"), was [designated](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iranian_supreme_leader_election "2026 Iranian supreme leader election") as Iran's new supreme leader.[378] Also on 8 March, US Central Command posted a message on [X](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/X_\(social_network\) "X \(social network\)") in Persian and Arabic which "strongly urge[s] civilians in Iran to stay at home" and alleging that "[t]he Iranian regime is knowingly endangering innocent lives by using heavily populated civilian areas to conduct military operations, including launching one-way attack drones and ballistic missiles".[379]
Israeli airstrikes hit some of Iran's oil storage facilities, killing four people.[380] Strikes on fuel depots near Tehran caused a "river of fire" to pour out along the streets in the surrounding area,[381] and the city became engulfed in a cloud of thick black smoke, causing toxic [acidic](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Acid_rain "Acid rain") black rain to fall in the surrounding area.[382] Residents were advised by Iranian authorities to stay indoors, wear masks when outside, and conserve food and fuel.[383] Kuwait reported that two of its officers were killed while performing duties.[384][385] The [Saudi Civil Defense](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saudi_Civil_Defense "Saudi Civil Defense") agency said that a strike on a compound in [Al-Kharj](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Al-Kharj "Al-Kharj") killed two people and injured 12 others.[386] [US Central Command](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/US_Central_Command "US Central Command") announced the death of a seventh US service member from an Iranian attack.[387]
The United Kingdom's Ministry of Defense confirmed that its forces had intercepted an Iranian drone headed towards Iraq. Additionally, the Ministry declared their intention to send a [Merlin helicopter](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Merlin_helicopter "Merlin helicopter") to the region to help aid in detecting future aerial threats.[388] According to the [Iranian Red Crescent Society](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iranian_Red_Crescent_Society "Iranian Red Crescent Society"), a total of 65 schools and 32 medical facilities (such as hospitals and pharmacies) have been targeted since the war started, and more than 10,000 civilian sites have been damaged.[389] On 9 March, NATO confirmed the interception of a second ballistic missile over Turkish air space,[390] landing in the area of [Gaziantep](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gaziantep "Gaziantep").[391] Meanwhile, Turkey deployed six F-16s and air defence systems to [Northern Cyprus](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Northern_Cyprus "Northern Cyprus") to ensure its security.[392]
Trump claimed that "the war is very complete, pretty much", after speaking with Russian president Vladimir Putin,[393] and said "we already won the war in many ways."[394] He additionally claimed that the Strait of Hormuz was open but also expressed interest in taking it over from Iran.[395] Trump later went back on some of his comments during a press conference held later that day, supporting Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth in stating that the war was just the "beginning of building a new country" and that it would end "soon".[396] However, Trump also said that the US will have the "most intense day" of strikes on Tuesday.[397] Following Syrian president Ahmed al-Sharaa's support for the Lebanon's objective to disarm Hezbollah,[398] the group [targeted](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_strikes_on_Syria_during_the_Iran_War "2026 strikes on Syria during the Iran War") the city of [Inkhil](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Inkhil "Inkhil") in Syria.[399] About a dozen [B1-B bombers](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rockwell_B-1_Lancer "Rockwell B-1 Lancer") arrived at bases in Europe. Five landed at the Fairford Royal Air Force base, UK, joining three [B-52 bombers](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/B-52_bombers "B-52 bombers"), while three others were redirected to [Ramstein Air Base](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ramstein_Air_Base "Ramstein Air Base"), Germany.[400]
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b1/Strait_of_Hormuz_Transits.webp/250px-Strait_of_Hormuz_Transits.webp.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Strait_of_Hormuz_Transits.webp)
Strait of Hormuz transits dropped significantly since the start of the war.[401]
[GPS jamming](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/GPS_jamming "GPS jamming") of uncertain origin has disrupted navigation of ships near the Strait of Hormuz, which may lead to disastrous accidents for oil tankers.[402] The [United States Department of State](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_States_Department_of_State "United States Department of State") (supported by [Saudi Arabia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saudi_Arabia "Saudi Arabia"))[403] designated the [Muslim Brotherhood](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Muslim_Brotherhood "Muslim Brotherhood") branch in [Sudan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sudan "Sudan") (a [Sudanese Islamist](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sudanese_Islamist "Sudanese Islamist") organization) as a [terrorist organization](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Terrorist_organization "Terrorist organization") due to its nexus with the [Axis of Resistance](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Axis_of_Resistance "Axis of Resistance").[404] Members of their militia fought alongside the IRGC due to the contacts established with the [Al-Bara' ibn Malik Battalion](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Al-Bara%27_ibn_Malik_Battalion "Al-Bara' ibn Malik Battalion") during the [Iranian intervention in Sudan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iranian_intervention_in_Sudan "Iranian intervention in Sudan").[405] On 10 March, a senior IRGC commander stated that Iran was only firing missiles with payloads of 1,000 kg or more.[406] AFP reported that Iran's attacks were the "most intense and heaviest" since the start of the war.[407] This shows a shift in tactics from saturating air defences to high-impact penetration strikes.[406]
Following the Syrian presidency's support for the disarmament of Hezbollah and a campaign of mobilization by the Syrian army on the Lebanese border,[398][408] shells from Lebanon landed in the town of [Serghaya](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Serghaya "Serghaya"), 20 km (12 mi) from Damascus where the [Syrian Armed Forces](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Syrian_Armed_Forces "Syrian Armed Forces") forces presided.[409] The strikes were confirmed by the Operations Authority of the [Syrian Army](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Syrian_Army "Syrian Army").[410] Later in the day, Israel issued an urgent evacuation notice to southern suburbs of Beirut.[411]
US admiral [Brad Cooper](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brad_Cooper_\(admiral\) "Brad Cooper \(admiral\)") reported that Iran's ballistic missile launch rate fell 90% from day one of the war and their drone launch rate fell 83%.[412] Defense analyst AJ Jaff reported that the missile launch rate fell 92% (480 to 40) and the drone launch rate fell 92% (720 to 60).[413] On 11 March, eleven B-1 and three B-52 US bombers were deployed to [RAF Fairford](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/RAF_Fairford "RAF Fairford") in the UK, where long-range photography showed bombs being prepared for loading. This followed US war secretary Pete Hegseth's statement that it was the "most intense day of strikes inside Iran", though the targets of the mission remain unknown.[414]
A branch of [Bank Sepah](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bank_Sepah "Bank Sepah") in Tehran was hit by a strike, prompting the IRGC to warn that in the future, they could retaliate by striking US or Israeli banks in the region.[415] Iran hit [Stryker](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Stryker_Corporation "Stryker Corporation"), a US-based medical supply company with a cyber attack.[416] On 12 March, [UNICEF](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/UNICEF "UNICEF") reported that more than 1,100 children were injured or killed (200 reportedly killed in Iran, 91 in Lebanon, 4 in Israel, and 1 in Kuwait), hundreds of thousands displaced, and millions unable to attend schools from the violence of the war since it started. It also noted that these numbers will likely increase.[417]
Iranian deputy [intelligence minister](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ministry_of_Intelligence_\(Iran\) "Ministry of Intelligence \(Iran\)") Akbar Ghaffari was killed in an airstrike.[418] An Iranian attack set two oil tankers on fire off the Iraqi coast, killing one person and prompting Iraq to suspend all oil terminal operations.[419] The historical [Safavid](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Safavid "Safavid")-era building of [Rashk-e Jenan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rashk-e_Jenan "Rashk-e Jenan"), also known as the Rashk Palace, was destroyed by direct US-Israeli strikes.[420] Other buildings that were damaged according to Ruhollah Seyed al-Asgari, the deputy director of cultural heritage for Isfahan province, include [Chehel Sotoun](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chehel_Sotoun "Chehel Sotoun") pavilion, the [Rakib Khaneh Mansion](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Rakib_Khaneh_Mansion&action=edit&redlink=1 "Rakib Khaneh Mansion \(page does not exist\)"), the [Timurid Hall](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Timurid_Hall&action=edit&redlink=1 "Timurid Hall \(page does not exist\)"), the [Ashraf Hall](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Ashraf_Hall&action=edit&redlink=1 "Ashraf Hall \(page does not exist\)") and the windows of [Ali Qapu](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ali_Qapu "Ali Qapu") palace.[421] Many of the affected buildings and monuments were legally protected by the [Blue Shield International](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Blue_Shield_International "Blue Shield International"),[422] which called damaging such sites a war crime.[423] Six French soldiers were injured in a drone strike at a base in Mala Qara, Iraqi Kurdistan. French president [Emmanuel Macron](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Emmanuel_Macron "Emmanuel Macron") announced on 13 March that one French soldier was killed in the attack.[424]
Also on 13 March, to help deal with the economic costs of the war, the US temporarily lifted restrictions on the sale of Russian oil. However, oil prices have barely been affected by this.[425] Iran announced a coordinated offensive by IRGC Navy, the Iranian Army, and Hezbollah against Israel and US bases.[426] Israel reported that 58 people have been hospitalized due to injuries following a missile strike.[425] Iran again targeted Gulf countries with missiles and drones, causing oil prices to rise. Bahrain reported attacks on fuel tanks. Two people were injured when a "hostile drone" struck a residential building in Kuwait. The Kuwaiti authorities also stated that defense equipment and the country's main international airport were attacked, and six power lines were reported to have gone out of service. AFP reported that explosions were heard in downtown Dubai. At the same time, the British Maritime Trade Operations Agency said a container ship was attacked 35 nautical miles (65 kilometers) north of Jebel Ali in the UAE. According to Al Jazeera Arabic, sirens went off in every city across Jordan.[427]
For the third time in the war, ballistic missiles entered Turkish air space and were intercepted, with explosions heard in the proximity of the city of [Adana](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Adana "Adana") as well as sirens from Incirlik Air Base.[428] Israel struck several IRGC checkpoints in Iran. According to [Reuters](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reuters "Reuters"), Israel attacked the checkpoints after receiving tip-offs by on the ground informants. Reuters also reported Israel had struck positions of the Basij used to suppress Iranian protests.[429]
### Third week (14–20 March)
Further information: [2026 Kharg Island attack](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Kharg_Island_attack "2026 Kharg Island attack"), [Assassination of Ali Larijani](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Assassination_of_Ali_Larijani "Assassination of Ali Larijani"), and [2026 South Pars field attack](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_South_Pars_field_attack "2026 South Pars field attack")
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c6/%D8%AC%D8%B2%DB%8C%D8%B1%D9%87_%D8%AE%D8%A7%D8%B1%DA%A9-_%D8%B7%D8%A8%DB%8C%D8%B9%D8%AA_%D9%88_%D8%B5%D9%86%D8%B9%D8%AA-10.jpg/250px-%D8%AC%D8%B2%DB%8C%D8%B1%D9%87_%D8%AE%D8%A7%D8%B1%DA%A9-_%D8%B7%D8%A8%DB%8C%D8%B9%D8%AA_%D9%88_%D8%B5%D9%86%D8%B9%D8%AA-10.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:%D8%AC%D8%B2%DB%8C%D8%B1%D9%87_%D8%AE%D8%A7%D8%B1%DA%A9-_%D8%B7%D8%A8%DB%8C%D8%B9%D8%AA_%D9%88_%D8%B5%D9%86%D8%B9%D8%AA-10.jpg)[Kharg Island](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kharg_Island "Kharg Island") in 2015, The fires on the towers are a normal part of oil refining.
As of the third week, analysts found little evidence of significant defections or desertions in the Iranian military;[aa] military defections have been considered a necessary condition for [regime change](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Regime_change "Regime change").[439] The US [conducted a large-scale bombing raid](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Kharg_Island_raid "2026 Kharg Island raid") on [Kharg Island](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kharg_Island "Kharg Island"), a key oil export hub home to 90 percent of Iran's oil exports. Over 90 military sites were targeted while the oil infrastructure was not targeted "for reasons of decency", according to Trump. Oil prices have risen over 40 percent since the start of the war.[440] According to CENTCOM, around 90 military targets were struck, while the island's oil infrastructure was not targeted.[441] On 14 March, [Kata'ib Hezbollah](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kata%27ib_Hezbollah "Kata'ib Hezbollah") security commander [Abu Ali al-Askari](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Abu_Ali_al-Askari&action=edit&redlink=1 "Abu Ali al-Askari \(page does not exist\)") was killed in an airstrike in Baghdad, according to the group.[442]
The first two floors of the [Royal Tulip Al Rasheed Hotel](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Royal_Tulip_Al_Rasheed_Hotel "Royal Tulip Al Rasheed Hotel") in Baghdad's [Green Zone](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Green_Zone "Green Zone") were hit by a drone whilst hosting a European Union and Saudi Arabian delegation. The attack was not claimed by anyone; however, due to the proximity of a similar attack that had happened 48 hours earlier on the US embassy in Baghdad, there were suspicions of it being an Iranian attack.[443]
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/40/Missile_interception_fragments_of_an_Iranian_missile_hit_Haifa_oil_refineries_in_Haifa_Bay%2C_March%2C_19_2026.jpg/250px-Missile_interception_fragments_of_an_Iranian_missile_hit_Haifa_oil_refineries_in_Haifa_Bay%2C_March%2C_19_2026.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Missile_interception_fragments_of_an_Iranian_missile_hit_Haifa_oil_refineries_in_Haifa_Bay,_March,_19_2026.jpg)An Iranian missile hit Haifa oil refineries in [Haifa Bay](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Haifa_Bay "Haifa Bay"), 19 March 2026
On 16 March, both China and US-aligned [NATO](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/NATO "NATO") nations in Europe rejected Trump's call to provide military support to reopen the [Strait of Hormuz](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Strait_of_Hormuz "Strait of Hormuz").[444][445] Trump rebuked his NATO allies, calling their decision a "very foolish mistake".[446] On 17 March, Trump made a statement on [Truth Social](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Truth_Social "Truth Social") renouncing NATO's assistance, and unexpectedly, US allies in the Indo-Pacific, namely Japan, South Korea, and [Australia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Australian_involvement_in_the_2026_Iran_war "Australian involvement in the 2026 Iran war"), for refusing to join US-led attacks on [Iran](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iran "Iran") during the war.[447][448][449][450] In the same statement, Trump declared that the United States "[does] not need the help of anyone" regarding the war.[451]
In the early morning of 17 March, several senior Iranian officials, including [Ali Larijani](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ali_Larijani "Ali Larijani") and Basij chief [Gholamreza Soleimani](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gholamreza_Soleimani "Gholamreza Soleimani") were [targeted by Israeli airstrikes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Assassination_of_Ali_Larijani "Assassination of Ali Larijani").[452][453] Both their deaths were later confirmed by Iranian state media.[454] In response, Iran launched a missile barrage that killed two Israeli civilians in [Ramat Gan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ramat_Gan "Ramat Gan").[455] The same day, Israel launched a ground invasion of [southern Lebanon](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Southern_Lebanon "Southern Lebanon").[456]
On 18 March, in a major escalation,[457] Israel [struck](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_South_Pars_field_attack "2026 South Pars field attack") the [South Pars natural gas field](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/South_Pars/North_Dome_Gas-Condensate_field "South Pars/North Dome Gas-Condensate field") in the Persian Gulf and its neighboring refineries in Iran with US coordination.[458] In retaliation, Iran attacked the world's largest LNG production facility in Qatar. Trump claimed he was not aware of the South Pars attack, promised that there would be no more Israeli attacks on Iranian oil infrastructure, and threatened Iran not to continue attacking Qatari energy infrastructure.[457][459] On the same day, Israel said it had killed Iranian intelligence minister [Esmaeil Khatib](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Esmaeil_Khatib "Esmaeil Khatib") in an overnight airstrike.[460] Pezeshkian confirmed Khatib's killing on the same day.[461] A hair salon in [Beit Awwa](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Beit_Awwa "Beit Awwa") was [struck by a projectile](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Beit_Awwa_salon_strike "2026 Beit Awwa salon strike"), killing four women and injuring six,[462] with Israeli authorities stating that it was most likely [cluster munitions](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cluster_munitions "Cluster munitions") and Palestinian authorities saying it was an errant Israeli interceptor.[463][464]
[](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:F-35_Lightning_II_fighter_jet_hit_over_Iran_2026.webm "Play video")[](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:F-35_Lightning_II_fighter_jet_hit_over_Iran_2026.webm)Video of suspected Iranian strike on a USAF F-35, released by [Tasnim News Agency](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tasnim_News_Agency "Tasnim News Agency"), 19 March 2026, the first case of a damaged stealth fighter in combat
On 19 March, a U.S. [F-35](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/F-35 "F-35") made an emergency landing after a suspected hit by Iranian forces.[465][466] A [BAZAN Group](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BAZAN_Group "BAZAN Group") refinery in Haifa was hit during a broader Iranian missile attack on the area as a retaliation for Israeli attacks on South Pars gas facilities.[467][468] Netanyahu insisted that "You can't make a revolution from the air" and that "there must be a ground component — I won't share with you all the options", implying that there was a potential for limited assault operations involving soldiers on the ground in Iran.[469] The U.S. began [an aerial campaign](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Strait_of_Hormuz_campaign "2026 Strait of Hormuz campaign") against Iranian vessels and drones in the Strait of Hormuz as part of an effort to reopen it to international shipping.[470]
### Fourth week (21–27 March)
Further information: [2026 Strait of Hormuz campaign](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Strait_of_Hormuz_campaign "2026 Strait of Hormuz campaign")
Admiral [Brad Cooper](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brad_Cooper_\(admiral\) "Brad Cooper \(admiral\)") said that the U.S. military had so far struck more than 8,000 Iranian military targets, including 130 vessels.[471] On 21 March, the U.S. conducted strikes on the [Natanz Nuclear Facility](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Natanz_Nuclear_Facility "Natanz Nuclear Facility") using [bunker buster](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bunker_buster "Bunker buster") bombs to target the site.[472] Russia condemned the strikes on Natanz as a "blatant violation of international law," while the [International Atomic Energy Agency](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/International_Atomic_Energy_Agency "International Atomic Energy Agency") (IAEA) urged military restraint "to avoid any risk of a nuclear accident."[473] In response, Iran struck the southern Israeli town of [Dimona](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dimona "Dimona"), injuring at least 78 people.[474] Iran said that it targeted the [Shimon Peres Negev Nuclear Research Center](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shimon_Peres_Negev_Nuclear_Research_Center "Shimon Peres Negev Nuclear Research Center").[475][476] Shortly after, Iran conducted another strike on the southern Israeli town of [Arad](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Arad,_Israel "Arad, Israel"), injuring over 116 people.[477]
Citing unnamed US officials, CNN and _The Wall Street Journal_ reported that Iran unsuccessfully attempted to strike the joint US–UK military base at [Diego Garcia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Diego_Garcia "Diego Garcia") on the [Chagos Islands](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chagos_Archipelago "Chagos Archipelago") in the [British Indian Ocean Territory](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/British_Indian_Ocean_Territory "British Indian Ocean Territory").[98] The report said one of the missiles broke apart mid-flight, while another was intercepted by a [SM-3](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/SM-3 "SM-3") air defence missile launched by a US warship.[478][479] Israel said that Iran used a two-stage [intercontinental ballistic missile](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Intercontinental_ballistic_missile "Intercontinental ballistic missile") to target the island.[480][481] Iran denied launching missiles towards Diego Garcia, saying that it was an Israeli false flag attack.[99] No evidence has been presented that the "alleged Iranian missiles" were nearing Diego Garcia or that one of the missiles was intercepted.[482]
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/bf/Operation-Roaring-Lion-0065.jpg/250px-Operation-Roaring-Lion-0065.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Operation-Roaring-Lion-0065.jpg)Israeli troops near the Lebanese border on 21 March 2026
The [Houthi movement](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Houthis "Houthis") in [Yemen](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yemen "Yemen") warned that it would respond to any escalation against Iran, including [efforts to reopen the Strait of Hormuz](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Strait_of_Hormuz_campaign "2026 Strait of Hormuz campaign"). It specifically warned the two Arab countries offering to join the Strait of Hormuz campaign—Bahrain and the United Arab Emirates—that they "will be the first to lose in this battle."[483] The Houthis further threatened to join the war in the event that US allies joined the attack on Iran or if the US and Israel used the [Red Sea](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Red_Sea "Red Sea") to carry out operations.[484] Lebanon's government expelled the Iranian ambassador.[485]
Trump issued an ultimatum to Iran, threatening to strike its [power plants](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Power_plants "Power plants") unless it opened the Strait of Hormuz within 48 hours.[486] Iran doubled down, threatening to "completely" close the Strait of Hormuz and strike vital infrastructure across the region such as energy and desalination facilities critical for drinking water.[487] [Iranian opposition](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iranian_opposition "Iranian opposition") figure and former crown prince [Reza Pahlavi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reza_Pahlavi "Reza Pahlavi") called on Trump and Netanyahu to target the military while sparing civilian infrastructure which "Iranians will need to rebuild our country."[488] Halfway through the deadline, Trump announced that he was postponing strikes against Iranian power plants for five days,[489] and said the US was negotiating with Iran to end the war.[490] Iran denied the talks ever took place or were taking place and called the president "deceitful".[491] Iran rejected the 15-point peace plan presented by the US.[492][493] Iran asserted that Lebanon must be included as part of a ceasefire deal, thereby making a ceasefire conditional on an end to the [2026 Lebanon war](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Lebanon_war "2026 Lebanon war") against Hezbollah.[494]
On 24 March, Israel or its allies shot down an Iranian missile crossing above Lebanon at high altitude, according to the Lebanese army, which also confirmed that the missile was not targeting Lebanon. This was the first time during the current conflict when a shootdown of an Iranian missile took place over Lebanese territory.[495][496]
A US strike on the Habbaniya military base—which hosts units from the [Popular Mobilization Forces](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Popular_Mobilization_Forces "Popular Mobilization Forces")—in [Anbar Governorate](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Al_Anbar_Governorate "Al Anbar Governorate") killed seven members of the [Iraqi Armed Forces](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iraqi_Armed_Forces "Iraqi Armed Forces") and injured 13 others. In response, Iraqi prime minister [Mohammed Shia' al-Sudani](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mohammed_Shia%27_al-Sudani "Mohammed Shia' al-Sudani") summoned the US embassy's [chargé d'affaires](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Charg%C3%A9_d%27affaires "Chargé d'affaires") in Baghdad.[497] Iranian state media threatened that Iran could seize Bahraini and Emirati territory if the US "makes any mistakes", while signaling its readiness to open a new front in Yemen by closing the [Bab al-Mandab](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bab-el-Mandeb "Bab-el-Mandeb") with the [Houthis](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Houthis "Houthis").[498][499] Israel said it killed Iran's top naval commander, [Alireza Tangsiri](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alireza_Tangsiri "Alireza Tangsiri"), purportedly as part of an effort to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.[500][501] According to Katz, Tangsiri was overseeing the [blockade of the Strait of Hormuz](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Strait_of_Hormuz_crisis "2026 Strait of Hormuz crisis") and was "directly responsible for the terrorist act of bombing and blocking the Strait of Hormuz."[502]
On 27 March, a day after Trump and Pete Hegseth announced the "neutralization" of Iran's military, an Iranian missile and drone strike on [Prince Sultan Air Base](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Prince_Sultan_Air_Base "Prince Sultan Air Base") damaged several US refueling aircraft and injured 29 US soldiers.[503] An [E-3 Sentry](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Boeing_E-3_Sentry "Boeing E-3 Sentry") was also damaged by the strike.[504][505][506] Military analyst Cedric Leighton described the attack as a "a serious blow to [US] surveillance capabilities."[507] Iran showed no signs of ceding its control over the Strait of Hormuz.[508] The same day, Iran blocked two Chinese ships from entering the strait and sought to formalize fees for ships passing through it.[509][510] Despite pushes for talks, Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz publicly vowed to "intensify and expand" Israeli attacks in Iran.[511] The same day, the [Shahid Khondab Heavy Water Complex](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IR-40 "IR-40") in Arak, which has in the past been used to test uranium enrichment, and the [Ardakan yellowcake production plant](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nuclear_facilities_in_Iran#Ardakan "Nuclear facilities in Iran") in Yazd Province were confirmed to have been damaged in Israeli airstrikes.[512][513]
### Fifth week (28 March – present)
Further information: [2026 Houthi strikes on Israel](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Houthi_strikes_on_Israel "2026 Houthi strikes on Israel")
The Houthis joined the war on 28 March [conducting a ballistic missile attack](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Houthi_strikes_on_Israel "2026 Houthi strikes on Israel") towards Israel and triggering air raid sirens in [Beersheba](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Beersheba "Beersheba").[514] The Houthis said that they had launched ballistic missiles targeting "sensitive" military sites in Israel, and vowed to continue the attacks "until the aggression on all resistance fronts stops." The missile was intercepted and no casualties were reported.[515] The Houthis subsequently launched a second missile at [Eilat](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eilat "Eilat").[516] In response, the internationally recognized [Yemeni government](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Presidential_Leadership_Council "Presidential Leadership Council") condemned Iran's "frequent attempts to drag Yemen" into conflict "through its terrorist militias."[517] On the same day, 2,500 U.S. marines from the [31st Marine Expeditionary Unit](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/31st_Marine_Expeditionary_Unit "31st Marine Expeditionary Unit") arrived in the Middle East. They are intended to be part of the effort to open the Strait of Hormuz.[518]
[U.S. Central Command](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_States_Central_Command "United States Central Command") stated that it had struck over 11,000 targets in Iran since the beginning of the war. On 28 March, joint U.S.–Israeli airstrikes severely damaged several residential and civilian facilities, including the [Iran University of Science and Technology](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iran_University_of_Science_and_Technology "Iran University of Science and Technology").[519] In response, Iran threatened to attack Israeli and American colleges across the Middle East.[520] Airstrikes also struck a 10,000-cubic-meter water reservoir in [Haftkel](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Haftkel "Haftkel"), in [Khuzestan province](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Khuzestan_province "Khuzestan province").[521]
On 29 March, joint U.S.–Israeli strikes bombed the port city of [Bandar Khamir](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bandar_Khamir "Bandar Khamir") in southern Iran, killing at least five people.[522] An Iranian missile struck a chemical plant in the [Ne'ot Hovav](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ne%27ot_Hovav "Ne'ot Hovav") industrial zone, injuring one person and causing fears of a hazardous leak.[523] A convoy of the Iraqi Popular Mobilization Forces was seen entering [Khorramshahr](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Khorramshahr "Khorramshahr") in southwestern Iran.[524] 11-years-old child soldier [Alireza Jafari](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alireza_Jafari_\(child_soldier\) "Alireza Jafari \(child soldier\)") died following an Israeli drone strike.[525]
On 30 March, Trump said on Truth Social that if a deal was not reached soon and the Strait of Hormuz reopened, the US would conclude the war by destroying all of Iran's power plants, oil wells, Kharg Island, and possibly all its desalinization plants.[526] On the same day Turkey and NATO intercepted the fourth missile directed towards Turkish territory. Iran denied firing a missile at Turkey, claiming that it was a [false flag](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/False_flag "False flag") attack.[527][528] The U.S. began flying [B-52 bombers](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Boeing_B-52_Stratofortress "Boeing B-52 Stratofortress") over Iranian territory for the first time. The U.S. used bombers to target an ammunition depot and air base in [Isfahan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Isfahan "Isfahan"), causing vast explosions.[529]

On 5 March 2026, a military source told [Fars News Agency](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fars_News_Agency "Fars News Agency") that Iran had fired over 500 ballistic and naval missiles and almost 2,000 drones since 28 February. The report claimed that almost 40 percent of the launches were aimed towards Israel, and almost 60 percent were fired towards US targets in the region.[530]
### Iran
See also: [Air campaign in Iranian Kurdistan (2026 Iran war)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Air_campaign_in_Iranian_Kurdistan_\(2026_Iran_war\) "Air campaign in Iranian Kurdistan \(2026 Iran war\)")
#### Assassination of Ali Khamenei
Main article: [Assassination of Ali Khamenei](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Assassination_of_Ali_Khamenei "Assassination of Ali Khamenei")
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/09/Ali_Khamenei_2026.02.12_%28cropped%29.jpg/250px-Ali_Khamenei_2026.02.12_%28cropped%29.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Ali_Khamenei_2026.02.12_\(cropped\).jpg)Khamenei on 12 February 2026, 16 days before his assassination
On 28 February 2026, [Ali Khamenei](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ali_Khamenei "Ali Khamenei"), the [supreme leader of Iran](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Supreme_leader_of_Iran "Supreme leader of Iran"), was assassinated in an Israeli air attack on his compound.[531] Early on 1 March, Iranian state media announced that Khamenei had been killed.[532] The [Fars News Agency](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fars_News_Agency "Fars News Agency"), which is controlled by the IRGC, announced that Khamenei's daughter, son-in-law, grandchild, and daughter-in-law [Zahra Haddad-Adel](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zahra_Haddad-Adel "Zahra Haddad-Adel") had also been killed in the strikes.[533][534][535] The Iranian government declared 40 days of mourning.[536]
