Iranian defense minister [Aziz Nasirzadeh](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aziz_Nasirzadeh "Aziz Nasirzadeh") and IRGC commander [Mohammad Pakpour](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mohammad_Pakpour "Mohammad Pakpour") were killed by Israeli airstrikes.[537][538] [Defence Council](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iranian_Defence_Council "Iranian Defence Council") secretary [Ali Shamkhani](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ali_Shamkhani "Ali Shamkhani") was killed,[539][540] along with four top [Ministry of Intelligence](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ministry_of_Intelligence_\(Iran\) "Ministry of Intelligence \(Iran\)") officials.[541] The IDF later stated that it had confirmed the deaths of seven Iranian security leaders, including Shamkhani, Nasirzadeh, and Pakpour.[542]
Additional senior officials confirmed to have been killed are [Salah Asadi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Salah_Asadi "Salah Asadi"), head of intelligence for Iran's emergency command, [Mohammad Shirazi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mohammad_Shirazi_\(general\) "Mohammad Shirazi \(general\)"), head of the military office of Khamenei, head of the [Organization of Defensive Innovation and Research](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Organization_of_Defensive_Innovation_and_Research "Organization of Defensive Innovation and Research") (SPND) [Hossein Jabal Amelian](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hossein_Jabal_Amelian "Hossein Jabal Amelian") and former SPND head [Reza Mozaffari Nia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reza_Mozaffari_Nia "Reza Mozaffari Nia").[235] On 1 March, chief of staff [Abdolrahim Mousavi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Abdolrahim_Mousavi "Abdolrahim Mousavi") and former president [Mahmoud Ahmadinejad](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mahmoud_Ahmadinejad "Mahmoud Ahmadinejad") were reported by Iranian state media to have also been killed by strikes.[543][544] However, later reports stated that Ahmadinejad is alive, or at least that reports of his death cannot be confirmed.[545]
On 28 February, intelligence and military sources told CBS News that 40 Iranian officials had been killed in the strikes but that they were not "clear whether these officials were in one location or multiple locations".[546][547] [Iran's Foreign Ministry](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ministry_of_Foreign_Affairs_\(Iran\) "Ministry of Foreign Affairs \(Iran\)") stated the military has lost control over several units, that are operating according to old general instructions.[548] On 13 March, the Israeli military estimated that between 3,000 and 4,000 Iranian soldiers and commanders had been killed.[549] On 18 March, the [Hengaw Organization for Human Rights](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hengaw_Organization_for_Human_Rights "Hengaw Organization for Human Rights") estimated that 5,300+ members of the Iranian military forces had been killed in the attacks, up from 1,300+ on 2 March, 2,100+ on 4 March, 3,900+ on 10 March, 4,400+ on 14 March, and 4,800+ on 17 March.[550]
On 17 March, Israel [assassinated](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Assassination_of_Ali_Larijani "Assassination of Ali Larijani") [Ali Larijani](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ali_Larijani "Ali Larijani"), [secretary](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Secretary_of_the_Supreme_National_Security_Council "Secretary of the Supreme National Security Council") of Iran's [Supreme National Security Council](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Supreme_National_Security_Council "Supreme National Security Council"). This was confirmed by Iran.[454] His assassination caused anxiety among Iranian officials who were concerned "that Israel would not stop until all of Iran's leaders were killed and the Islamic Republic toppled", as well as questions over who would be targeted next.[551]
The NGO [HRANA](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/HRANA "HRANA") documented 3,114 deaths in Iran due to airstrikes by 17 March, including 1,354 civilians, 1,138 military personnel, and 622 unclassified. HRANA states that "military casualties are significantly higher than the figures reported in these reports," as confirmations depend largely on government data which is obscured "due to the sensitive nature of military information." Because of HRANA's "limited access to locations where military forces are present, [its] count largely reflects reports of senior officers or military personnel who were present in urban areas."[552][ab] On 31 March, Iran International reported that at least 4,700 security forces had been killed.[555]
#### Civilians
Further information: [2026 Minab school attack](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Minab_school_attack "2026 Minab school attack")
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1a/Shajareh_Tayyebeh_school_in_Minab_photos_from_Mehr_%283%29.jpg/250px-Shajareh_Tayyebeh_school_in_Minab_photos_from_Mehr_%283%29.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Shajareh_Tayyebeh_school_in_Minab_photos_from_Mehr_\(3\).jpg)Rescuers and residents searching through the rubble of the [Minab girls' elementary school](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Minab_school_attack "2026 Minab school attack"), destroyed by the United States
On 28 February, the Shajareh Tayyebeh girl's elementary school in [Minab](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Minab "Minab") was [hit by the American attacks](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Minab_school_attack "2026 Minab school attack"), according to Iran.[556] The school was located close to[ac] the Sayyid al-Shuhada military complex,[560] of which it once had been a part.[559][561] While there is no independent confirmation of the number killed,[562][563] Iranian state media reported that more than 175 people, mostly school children, were killed and 95 wounded in the strike.[564] Evidence assembled by US investigators[565][566] and _The New York Times_ suggested that the attack was carried out by the US.[567] A video showed a US Tomahawk missile hitting a building adjacent to the school, adding to evidence indicating the US was responsible for the strike.[568] On 11 March, _[The New York Times](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_New_York_Times "The New York Times")_ reported that preliminary findings in an ongoing investigation indicated that the strike was conducted by the US with outdated targeting data.[569] UN human rights experts characterized the strike as a potential [war crime](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_States_war_crimes "United States war crimes") under the [Rome Statute](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rome_Statute "Rome Statute").[570]
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/98/Damages_caused_by_attacks_on_Tehran_22_Avash_25_esfand_1404.webp/250px-Damages_caused_by_attacks_on_Tehran_22_Avash_25_esfand_1404.webp.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Damages_caused_by_attacks_on_Tehran_22_Avash_25_esfand_1404.webp)Damage to a residential structure in Tehran following aerial attacks on 15 March 2026
At least 20 civilians, mostly families who gathered after breaking their [fast](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Islamic_fasting "Islamic fasting") at Ramadan,[571] were killed in Tehran's Niloofar Square on 1 or 2 March, according to Iranian-state owned media.[572] On 3 March, the Red Crescent said that over 600 civilians had been killed,[573] while the [Human Rights Activists in Iran](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Human_Rights_Activists_in_Iran "Human Rights Activists in Iran") estimated that 742 civilians had been killed in the attacks.[552] On 7 March, the [Iranian Red Crescent Society](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iranian_Red_Crescent_Society "Iranian Red Crescent Society") reported that over 6,668 civilian units were targeted by US-Israeli strikes, including:[574] 5,535 residential units, 1,041 commercial units, 14 medical centres, 65 schools, and 13 centres affiliated with the Iranian Red Crescent Society. The US reportedly used [double tap](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Double_tap_strike "Double tap strike") airstrikes to maximize casualties.[575][576] The house of the acclaimed filmmaker [Abbas Kiarostami](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Abbas_Kiarostami "Abbas Kiarostami") was reportedly damaged on 25 March.[577] As of 23 March, HRANA estimated that at least 15% of the total human casualties had been under the age of 18.[578]
An Israeli airstrike on 9 March in Resalat neighbourhood of Tehran destroyed a Basij-affiliated building as well as three residential buildings, resulting in 40 to 50 deaths. According to a BBC analysis, Israel used [Mark 82](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mark_82 "Mark 82") bombs in the attack.[571]
As of 27 March, Iran reports damage to at least 120 [historical sites](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/History_of_Iran "History of Iran") by US-Israeli strikes.[579]
An analysis by BBC Verify suggest that the deadly attack on residential buildings and a sports hall in the town of [Lamerd](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lamerd "Lamerd") had been performed by a [Precision Strike Missile](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Precision_Strike_Missile "Precision Strike Missile") (PrSM), a state-of-the-art US munition.[580]
#### Institutions
The opening attack on 28 February targeted the compound of Leadership House. On 2 March 2026, video footage released by Iran International showed the IRGC Malek-Ashtar building in Tehran completely destroyed following a joint US-Israel missile strike on the capital.[581] In the early hours of 3 March 2026, the complex of the [Islamic Republic of Iran Broadcasting](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Islamic_Republic_of_Iran_Broadcasting "Islamic Republic of Iran Broadcasting") (IRIB), the state broadcaster's headquarters in Tehran, was hit in a separate Israeli air operation.[582][583]
Iranian state-linked media indicated that Iran's parliament building was also targeted by airstrikes.[584] Trump told reporters that "Just about everything's been knocked out" and that Iran had no navy, air force, air detection, or radar after the attacks.[585] On 3 March, Israel bombed Iran's 84-member [Assembly of Experts](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Assembly_of_Experts "Assembly of Experts") as [they were in a preliminary meeting to elect the next supreme leader](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iranian_supreme_leader_election "2026 Iranian supreme leader election")[586][587] on 8 March.[588][589]
#### Infrastructure
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/4b/Attack_on_Gandhi_Hospital_3.jpg/250px-Attack_on_Gandhi_Hospital_3.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Attack_on_Gandhi_Hospital_3.jpg)Tehran's Gandhi Hospital was damaged on 1 March 2026, during a wave of US and Israeli airstrikes targeting the Iranian capital.
By 5 March, the World Health Organization had identified 13 Iranian health infrastructure sites that were hit during the war, as well as one health facility in Lebanon.[590] The Iranian Foreign Minister said that the US [had struck a desalination plant](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Qeshm_Island_desalination_plant_attack "2026 Qeshm Island desalination plant attack") on [Qeshm Island](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Qeshm_Island "Qeshm Island"), disrupting clean water supplies to 30 villages, but both the US and Israel denied making such an attack.[591][382] The [Russian consulate in Isfahan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_diplomatic_missions_of_Russia#Asia "List of diplomatic missions of Russia") was damaged by the US-Israel airstrikes.[592]
### United States
On 1 March, six US soldiers were killed and more than 30 were injured in an Iranian [drone attack](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Port_Shuaiba_drone_attack "2026 Port Shuaiba drone attack") against a US military installation near [Camp Arifjan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Camp_Arifjan "Camp Arifjan") in Port Shuaiba, [Kuwait](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kuwait "Kuwait"). All of those killed were soldiers of the [United States Army Reserve](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_States_Army_Reserve "United States Army Reserve") assigned to the [103rd Expeditionary Sustainment Command](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/103rd_Expeditionary_Sustainment_Command "103rd Expeditionary Sustainment Command"), headquartered in [Des Moines, Iowa](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Des_Moines,_Iowa "Des Moines, Iowa").[593][594][595][596][597] On 4 March, US Marine Corps Lance Corporal Kevin Melendez died in Saudi Arabia in a non-hostile incident.[598] On 8 March, CENTCOM reported a National Guard soldier died from a "health-related incident in Kuwait on March 6 during a medical emergency".[599] Sgt. Benjamin N. Pennington, a soldier of the [1st Space Brigade](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1st_Space_Brigade "1st Space Brigade") wounded in an attack against [Prince Sultan Air Base](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Prince_Sultan_Air_Base "Prince Sultan Air Base") in Saudi Arabia on 1 March, died from his injuries on 9 March.[600][601][602]
Six American military airmen were killed on 12 March when their KC-135 aerial refueling aircraft crashed in western Iraq while supporting US military operations. CENTCOM said that the crash was not the result of hostile action and that it had resulted during an incident involving another US aircraft inside "friendly airspace". By 13 March, the US military publicly announced that about 140 American servicemen had been injured during the war.[603]
The [USS  _Gerald R. Ford_](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/USS_Gerald_R._Ford "USS Gerald R. Ford"), the largest aircraft carrier in the theatre, was damaged by a fire that broke out 12 March. The [U.S. Navy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/U.S._Navy "U.S. Navy") said the fire, which injured multiple[ad] sailors, started in a laundry area and was not combat-related.[608] The carrier had already been dealing with other mechanical issues, including a malfunctioning sewage treatment system, since before it arrived in the Middle East.[609] According to multiple reports on 17–18 March, _Gerald R. Ford_ paused launching airstrikes and sailed toward the [Crete Naval Base](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Crete_Naval_Base "Crete Naval Base") for repairs.[605][607][610]
### Israel
Main article: [2026 Iranian strikes on Israel](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iranian_strikes_on_Israel "2026 Iranian strikes on Israel")
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/ea/Dankal_shelter_2.jpg/250px-Dankal_shelter_2.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Dankal_shelter_2.jpg)Civilians sheltering in a converted light rail station in Israel [](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Civil_defense_siren_and_missile_explosions_sounds_in_Israel_during_Iran_war_2026.ogg "Play audio")Duration: 1 minute and 51 seconds.1:51[](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Civil_defense_siren_and_missile_explosions_sounds_in_Israel_during_Iran_war_2026.ogg)Civil defense siren and missile explosions sounds in central Israel, 5 March 2026
The first connected Iranian attack struck a residential building in Israel, leaving one Israeli civilian injured.[611] [Magen David Adom](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Magen_David_Adom "Magen David Adom") reported that Iran's initial attacks had left 89 injured, with three directly wounded and the rest indirectly, most of them civilians.[612] On 28 February, a direct hit in [Tel Aviv](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tel_Aviv "Tel Aviv") killed a civilian woman and injured 22 others, one seriously.[613] On 1 March, an Iranian strike hit a synagogue and residential buildings in [Beit Shemesh](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Beit_Shemesh "Beit Shemesh"), killing nine people and injuring 49 others.[614] On 9 March, two workers were killed in [Yehud](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yehud,_Israel "Yehud, Israel"), outside of Tel Aviv, after being struck by submunitions from an Iranian missile equipped with a [cluster bomb](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cluster_bomb "Cluster bomb") warhead while working outdoors at a construction site.[615][616]
On 17 March, a cluster munition from the warhead of an Iranian missile hit an apartment building in [Ramat Gan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ramat_Gan "Ramat Gan") and killed two residents in their 70s, who were found just outside their safe room.[617] A statement from the [Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Islamic_Revolutionary_Guard_Corps "Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps") called the barrage that killed the two civilians "revenge for the blood of martyr Dr. [Ali Larijani](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ali_Larijani "Ali Larijani") and his companions", who had been killed a day before the Iranian attack.[618] According to [CNN](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CNN "CNN"), the use of cluster bombs is against international law, with N.R. Jenzen-Jones of [Armament Research Services](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Armament_Research_Services "Armament Research Services") telling CNN that this type of warhead is being used "primarily to sow terror amongst a civilian population."[619] The executive director of the [Arms Control Association](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Arms_Control_Association "Arms Control Association") called the Iranian targeting of cluster munition warheads at residential sections of [Israel](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Israel "Israel") during the war as deliberate, saying that "Iran appears to be launching them into relatively populated areas, probably with the goal of producing potential civilian harm."[620] Neither Iran nor Israel is party to the [Convention on Cluster Munitions](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Convention_on_Cluster_Munitions "Convention on Cluster Munitions"),[621] and both Israel and Iran are among 17 countries that either produce cluster munitions, or reserve the right to do so.[622]
Israeli broadcaster [Kan 11](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kan_11 "Kan 11") reported that 90, 65, 25, and then 20 missiles were launched at Israel from Iran on each of the first four days of the war.[623] Although the number of missiles in each volley has decreased, the missiles themselves have increased in size and impact.[624] On 9 March, Israel said that Lebanon has made more attacks against them than Iran has over the past week.[625]
### Lebanon
Main article: [2026 Lebanon war](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Lebanon_war "2026 Lebanon war")
At least 1,001 people were killed and 2,584 more were wounded by Israeli strikes in Lebanon,[626][627] and Lebanon states more than one million[628] Lebanese nationals, corresponding to 1/6th of the country,[629] were displaced.[355][630] Youssef Ragi, the Lebanese foreign minister, announced that Lebanon's Council of Ministers decided to ban the military and security of Hezbollah, and called for them to hand over their weapons.[631] Israel called for Lebanon to take action beyond mere statements,[632] and Katz said that if they don't, then Israel will invade Lebanon and do this themselves.[633] An NBC News analysis described Hezbollah as "significantly weakened".[631] Sami Nader, the director of the Institute of Political Science at [Saint Joseph University of Beirut](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Joseph_University_of_Beirut "Saint Joseph University of Beirut"), described the situation as a "tipping point".[634][635] Israel's army chief of staff [Eyal Zamir](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eyal_Zamir "Eyal Zamir") said they will "deepen the operation" in Lebanon.[636]
### Iraq
Main articles: [Iraq in the 2026 Iran war](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iraq_in_the_2026_Iran_war "Iraq in the 2026 Iran war") and [2026 Iranian strikes on the Kurdistan Region](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iranian_strikes_on_the_Kurdistan_Region "2026 Iranian strikes on the Kurdistan Region")
In Iraq, two members of the Iran-backed [Popular Mobilization Forces](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Popular_Mobilization_Forces "Popular Mobilization Forces") (PMF) were killed and three injured in a US-Israeli attack on 28 February.[637] A later report from _The New York Times_ estimated three dead.[638] On 1 and 2 March, the United States and Israel conducted several attacks on Iranian-backed Iraqi militias to degrade their military capabilities to conduct retaliatory attacks.[639][640]
### Iranian strikes on other countries and regions
Further information: [2026 Iranian strikes on Arab countries](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iranian_strikes_on_Arab_countries "2026 Iranian strikes on Arab countries")
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/8e/Drone_attacks_and_strikes_in_the_UAE.webp/250px-Drone_attacks_and_strikes_in_the_UAE.webp.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Drone_attacks_and_strikes_in_the_UAE.webp)[Drone attacks and strikes in the UAE](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iranian_strikes_on_the_United_Arab_Emirates "2026 Iranian strikes on the United Arab Emirates") [![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/66/EAS_warning_sent_to_phones_during_the_Iranian_stikes.png/250px-EAS_warning_sent_to_phones_during_the_Iranian_stikes.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:EAS_warning_sent_to_phones_during_the_Iranian_stikes.png)An emergency alert sent to UAE residents shortly before Dubai International Airport was struck at around 12:30 A.M. local time, 28 February[641]
In addition to its strikes against Israel, Iran launched strikes against multiple Gulf states. Iranian strikes against [Bahrain](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iranian_strikes_on_Bahrain "2026 Iranian strikes on Bahrain") killed three people.[642][643] Strikes against [Kuwait](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iranian_strikes_on_Kuwait "2026 Iranian strikes on Kuwait") killed four soldiers and four civilians.[644] Strikes against [Oman](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iranian_strikes_on_Oman "2026 Iranian strikes on Oman") killed three people.[645] Strikes against [Saudi Arabia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iranian_strikes_on_Saudi_Arabia "2026 Iranian strikes on Saudi Arabia") killed 2 people.[646][647] Strikes against the [United Arab Emirates](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iranian_strikes_on_the_United_Arab_Emirates "2026 Iranian strikes on the United Arab Emirates") killed two soldiers and six civilians.[648]
There were also strikes in [Qatar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iranian_strikes_on_Qatar "2026 Iranian strikes on Qatar"),[649][650] [Azerbaijan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iran_strikes_on_Azerbaijan "2026 Iran strikes on Azerbaijan"),[356][357] and the [Akrotiri and Dhekelia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iranian_strikes_on_Akrotiri_and_Dhekelia "2026 Iranian strikes on Akrotiri and Dhekelia") British Overseas Territory on [Cyprus](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iranian_strikes_on_Cyprus "2026 Iranian strikes on Cyprus").[651]
Also the Iranian attacks affected military infrastructure from [NATO](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/NATO "NATO") countries in the region, "including an Italian installation in Iraq and Kuwait, and a French naval facility in the United Arab Emirates."[652]
