## Impact
### In Iran
#### Leadership
See also: [2026 Iranian supreme leader election](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iranian_supreme_leader_election "2026 Iranian supreme leader election")
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/47/Masoud_Pezeshkian_2025_%28cropped%29.jpg/120px-Masoud_Pezeshkian_2025_%28cropped%29.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Masoud_Pezeshkian_2025_\(cropped\).jpg)
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/2b/Gholam_Hossein_Mohseni-Eje%27i_2023_%28Cropped%29.jpg/120px-Gholam_Hossein_Mohseni-Eje%27i_2023_%28Cropped%29.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Gholam_Hossein_Mohseni-Eje%27i_2023_\(Cropped\).jpg)
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/ef/Alireza_Arafi_13990824_0146818_crop.jpg/120px-Alireza_Arafi_13990824_0146818_crop.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Alireza_Arafi_13990824_0146818_crop.jpg)
The three members of the [Interim Leadership Council](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Interim_Leadership_Council "Interim Leadership Council") – President [Masoud Pezeshkian](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Masoud_Pezeshkian "Masoud Pezeshkian"), Chief Justice [Gholam-Hossein Mohseni-Eje'i](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gholam-Hossein_Mohseni-Eje%27i "Gholam-Hossein Mohseni-Eje'i") and Ayatollah [Alireza Arafi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alireza_Arafi "Alireza Arafi")
The death of Ali Khamenei triggered an election for a new supreme leader. Under the terms of Iran's constitution, an [Interim Leadership Council](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Interim_Leadership_Council "Interim Leadership Council") was established on 1 March to exercise the functions of Iran's [head of state](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Head_of_state "Head of state") until a new supreme leader is elected.[265]
[Mojtaba Khamenei](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mojtaba_Khamenei "Mojtaba Khamenei") was elected on 8 March 2026 to replace his father as supreme leader,[700] and the IRGC, as well as Iran's top leaders (including [Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mohammad_Bagher_Ghalibaf "Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf"), [Ali Larijani](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ali_Larijani "Ali Larijani"), and [Masoud Pezeshkian](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Masoud_Pezeshkian "Masoud Pezeshkian")[701]) pledged their allegiance to him.[702]
In late March the NYT described Iran's leadership as paralyzed with severely disrupted decision-making process. It also reported that the damage to communications infrastructure caused paranoia and internal power struggles.[703]
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/42/Tehran_-_The_Fourth_Day_of_War_4_Avash.webp/250px-Tehran_-_The_Fourth_Day_of_War_4_Avash.webp.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Tehran_-_The_Fourth_Day_of_War_4_Avash.webp)Residents in Tehran on the third day of US-Israeli air strikes, 3 March 2026
#### Civilian life
See also: [2025–2026 Iranian protests § After the strikes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2025%E2%80%932026_Iranian_protests#After_the_strikes "2025–2026 Iranian protests")
As the US and Israel executed joint strikes targeting Iranian military and institutional targets, Iran's capital reportedly became a [ghost town](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ghost_town "Ghost town"), as civilians feared going outside or to their daily jobs.[704] Reports have emerged that prisoners in [Evin Prison](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Evin_Prison "Evin Prison") have been receiving limited bread and water since the onset of the war.[705] After the strikes, schools were ordered closed while banks and government offices remained in operation at reduced capacity. Local media reported that subway and bus services would remain operational.[706]
Amid the renewed "near total" [internet blackout in Iran](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Internet_blackout_in_Iran "2026 Internet blackout in Iran"), [NetBlocks](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/NetBlocks "NetBlocks") reported that internet connectivity in Iran dropped to 4% of ordinary levels.[563][707][708] While the internet blackout has exceeded 240 hours, making it the second longest ever, the government started handing out "white sim cards", which bypass filters, to government supporters, in order to allow them to promote its messaging.[709][243] As news of Khamenei's death broke, security forces were deployed to prevent an uprising, with footage showing them opening fire on celebrants in the streets[710][711][712] and shooting at people chanting behind the windows of their homes.[713]
Civilians in Tehran used tapes on their glass windows to prevent them breaking from the shock waves of the airstrikes, a practice common also during the [War of the Cities](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/War_of_the_Cities "War of the Cities") of the 1980s.[577]
During the first two weeks of March 2026, over 70,000 [Afghan refugees](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Afghans_in_Iran "Afghans in Iran") returned to Afghanistan, frequently under forced circumstances, exacerbating the country's existing [severe humanitarian crisis](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2025%E2%80%932026_hunger_crisis_in_Afghanistan "2025–2026 hunger crisis in Afghanistan").[714] Arafat Jamal, the UNHCR Representative in Afghanistan, described returnees as "fleeing one war only to encounter another," noting that they faced limited options amid heightened [border tensions](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Afghanistan%E2%80%93Pakistan_conflict "2026 Afghanistan–Pakistan conflict") and military confrontations between Afghanistan and Pakistan.[715]
#### Military
On 26 March, it was reported that children as young as 12 years of age may join Iran's war support, leading to concerns of the use of [child soldiers](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Child_soldiers "Child soldiers").[716][717][718] Days later, rights groups stated an 11-year-old was killed while on duty at a Tehran checkpoint.[719]
On 29 March, footage showed Iraq's [Popular Mobilization Forces](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Popular_Mobilization_Forces "Popular Mobilization Forces") deployed in Iran.[720][721][722]
#### Kurdish–Iranian crisis
Further information: [2026 Kurdish–Iranian crisis](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Kurdish%E2%80%93Iranian_crisis "2026 Kurdish–Iranian crisis")
According to _[The Wall Street Journal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Wall_Street_Journal "The Wall Street Journal")_ , Trump had been open to supporting armed militias, particularly [Kurds in Iran](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kurds_in_Iran "Kurds in Iran") that have been [in armed conflict with Tehran for decades](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kurdish_separatism_in_Iran "Kurdish separatism in Iran")[330] and have historically been a regional ally of the United States.[723] However, Trump later said he had ruled out sending the Kurds to Iran.[724]
#### Cultural heritage sites
Several historic and cultural sites, including [UNESCO World Heritage Sites](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/UNESCO_World_Heritage_Sites "UNESCO World Heritage Sites"), were damaged during the war. On 2 March, a strike on [Arg Square](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Arg_Square "Arg Square") damaged nearby [Golestan Palace](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Golestan_Palace "Golestan Palace"), prompting [UNESCO](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/UNESCO "UNESCO") to issue a statement of concern.[725][726] On 5 March, the [Azadi Sport Complex](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Azadi_Sport_Complex "Azadi Sport Complex") was bombed.[727] An 8 March strike on [Falak-ol-Aflak](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Falak-ol-Aflak "Falak-ol-Aflak"), which was marked with a [blue shield emblem](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hague_Convention_for_the_Protection_of_Cultural_Property_in_the_Event_of_Armed_Conflict "Hague Convention for the Protection of Cultural Property in the Event of Armed Conflict"), damaged several sections of the site.[728] Strikes on [Isfahan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Isfahan "Isfahan") on 9 March damaged [Naqsh-e Jahan Square](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Naqsh-e_Jahan_Square "Naqsh-e Jahan Square"), [Chehel Sotoun](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chehel_Sotoun "Chehel Sotoun"), [Ali Qapu](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ali_Qapu "Ali Qapu"), the [Shah Mosque](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shah_Mosque_\(Isfahan\) "Shah Mosque \(Isfahan\)"), [Jameh Mosque](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jameh_Mosque_of_Isfahan "Jameh Mosque of Isfahan"), and [Teymouri Hall](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Natural_History_Museum_of_Isfahan "Natural History Museum of Isfahan").[729][728] On 11 March, UNESCO urged protection for Iran's [heritage sites](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Heritage_sites "Heritage sites") and World Heritage Sites that have been damaged or are under high risk due to the war, alongside other [historic sites](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Historic_site "Historic site") in Israel, Lebanon, and across the Middle East.[730]
### In Israel
Israel declared a nationwide [state of emergency](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/State_of_emergency "State of emergency"), saying that the strikes on Iran were the largest that it had ever launched.[245][731] Israeli schools and workspaces were closed and public gatherings were canceled.[732]
### In Palestine
See also: [Gaza war](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gaza_war "Gaza war")
The Israeli [Coordinator of Government Activities in the Territories](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Coordinator_of_Government_Activities_in_the_Territories "Coordinator of Government Activities in the Territories") closed multiple aid crossings in the [Gaza Strip](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gaza_Strip "Gaza Strip") (specifically interrupting free circulation on the [Egypt–Gaza border](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Egypt%E2%80%93Gaza_border "Egypt–Gaza border")).[733] Israeli forces suspended [United Nations](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_Nations "United Nations") humanitarian movements and postponed planned rotations of international humanitarian staff (including medical evacuations and the return of people into Gaza), which the [United Nations Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_Nations_Office_for_the_Coordination_of_Humanitarian_Affairs "United Nations Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs") condemned.[734]
[Israeli settler violence](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Israeli_settler_violence "Israeli settler violence") against [Palestinians](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Palestinians "Palestinians") in [occupied West Bank](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Occupied_West_Bank "Occupied West Bank") has increased; settlers have taken advantage of curbs on movement imposed during the renewed [Israeli state of emergency](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Israeli_state_of_emergency "Israeli state of emergency"). The renewed blocking of West Bank roads (in the context of the [2026 Lebanon war](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Lebanon_war "2026 Lebanon war")) has delayed medics from assisting injured Palestinians.[735] The EU stated on 10 March that the Israeli actions were unacceptable and urged the Israeli authorities to make immediate efforts to prevent the violence against Palestinian civilians under [international law](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/International_law "International law"), warning that "impunity for such acts risks provoking further violence".[736]
### In Gulf states
The [Arab states of the Persian Gulf](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Arab_states_of_the_Persian_Gulf "Arab states of the Persian Gulf") started a campaign of prosecution against civilians and foreign citizens that shared or reposted rumours from unverified sources about the Iranian attacks, with the stated main goal to curb misinformation that could harm public order.[737] Bahrain's Cyber Crime Directorate also started a campaign of arresting civilians in the country, which has a large [Shiite minority](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shia_in_Bahrain "Shia in Bahrain"), who allegedly expressed "their support for the Iranian shelling targeting the Kingdom of Bahrain", describing such activities as treason and as promotion of hostile acts against the state.[738] Trump said "Even if we knew Gulf countries would be hit, big deal we did what we have to do".[636]
### Economic and geopolitical impact
Main articles: [Economic impact of the 2026 Iran war](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Economic_impact_of_the_2026_Iran_war "Economic impact of the 2026 Iran war"), [2026 Iran war regional mobilizations](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iran_war_regional_mobilizations "2026 Iran war regional mobilizations"), and [Evacuations during the 2026 Iran war](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Evacuations_during_the_2026_Iran_war "Evacuations during the 2026 Iran war")
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/21/2026-03-03_Treibstoffpreise_nach_Beginn_von_%22Epic_Fury%22_HOF8332_RAW-Export_cens.png/120px-2026-03-03_Treibstoffpreise_nach_Beginn_von_%22Epic_Fury%22_HOF8332_RAW-Export_cens.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:2026-03-03_Treibstoffpreise_nach_Beginn_von_%22Epic_Fury%22_HOF8332_RAW-Export_cens.png)
Petrol gas prices measured in [euros](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Euro "Euro") and in [liters](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Liter "Liter") in Germany on 3 March 2026
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a7/2026-03-08_Spritpreise_an_der_Hofer_Lutherkirche_20260308_160051.jpg/120px-2026-03-08_Spritpreise_an_der_Hofer_Lutherkirche_20260308_160051.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:2026-03-08_Spritpreise_an_der_Hofer_Lutherkirche_20260308_160051.jpg)
Petrol gas prices measured in [euros](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Euro "Euro") and in [liters](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Liter "Liter") in Germany on 8 March 2026, note the increase in price compared to a week earlier.
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/67/20260309_DM_MiddleEast_Repatriations%281%29.pdf/page1-250px-20260309_DM_MiddleEast_Repatriations%281%29.pdf.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:20260309_DM_MiddleEast_Repatriations\(1\).pdf)Map of emergency evacuation of persons to Europe [![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/91/Crisis_of_dragon%27s_blood_at_M%E1%BA%A1c_Th%C3%A1i_T%C3%B4ng_Street_10-03-2026_E6.jpg/250px-Crisis_of_dragon%27s_blood_at_M%E1%BA%A1c_Th%C3%A1i_T%C3%B4ng_Street_10-03-2026_E6.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Crisis_of_dragon%27s_blood_at_M%E1%BA%A1c_Th%C3%A1i_T%C3%B4ng_Street_10-03-2026_E6.jpg)Gas lines in [Hanoi, Vietnam](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hanoi,_Vietnam "Hanoi, Vietnam"). Nearly 90% of the oil transiting the Strait of Hormuz is destined for places in [East](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Far_East "Far East") and [South Asia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/South_Asia "South Asia").
The war has global economic consequences: The conflict led to immediate surges in [oil](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Price_of_oil "Price of oil") and [gas prices](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Natural_gas_prices "Natural gas prices"), widespread disruptions in aviation and tourism, and heightened volatility in financial markets.[739][740] The head of [International Energy Agency](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/International_Energy_Agency "International Energy Agency") (IEA) described the situation caused by the war as the "greatest global energy security challenge in history".[741]
#### Disruption of international trade
Analysts projected potential global inflation increases and risks of recession if disruptions persisted, particularly through [closures](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Strait_of_Hormuz_crisis "2026 Strait of Hormuz crisis") of key shipping routes like the [Strait of Hormuz](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Strait_of_Hormuz "Strait of Hormuz") and strikes on gas and oil facilities.[739][740] Oil prices rose following a reduction in traffic through the strait, and strikes on oil facilities in countries such as Saudi Arabia and the UAE.[742] Oil prices went over US$100 per barrel[670] to $114 per barrel,[670] the highest prices since the [COVID-19 pandemic](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic "COVID-19 pandemic"). An expert warned that if this continues, this could lead into a worldwide [economic recession](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Economic_recession "Economic recession").[743] Gas prices also rose,[290] and analysts said raising the world's prices was a part of the Iranian government's plan to apply pressure on the world to stop the war.[744] Toward the end of March there were significant shortages of domestic [petroleum products](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Petroleum_product "Petroleum product") in [southeast Asia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Southeast_Asia "Southeast Asia").[745]
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/28/USS_Bainbridge_%28DDG_96%29_transits_the_Suez_Canal_during_Operation_Epic_Fury_%289552415%29.jpg/250px-USS_Bainbridge_%28DDG_96%29_transits_the_Suez_Canal_during_Operation_Epic_Fury_%289552415%29.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:USS_Bainbridge_\(DDG_96\)_transits_the_Suez_Canal_during_Operation_Epic_Fury_\(9552415\).jpg)The guided-missile destroyer [USS  _Bainbridge_](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/USS_Bainbridge_\(DDG-96\) "USS Bainbridge \(DDG-96\)") passes through the [Suez Canal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Suez_Canal "Suez Canal") during Operation Epic Fury, March 2026
On 6 March, Qatar's energy minister [Saad al-Kaabi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saad_al-Kaabi "Saad al-Kaabi") warned that if the war continues other Gulf energy producers may be forced to halt exports and declare [force majeure](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Force_majeure "Force majeure"), and that "this will bring down economies of the world".[746] Stock markets experienced declines, with the [Dow Jones Industrial Average](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dow_Jones_Industrial_Average "Dow Jones Industrial Average") falling over 400 points (one percent) on 2 March.[747] Broader economic forecasts warned of inflationary pressures and slowed global growth if the conflict prolonged.[748]
The UN [World Food Programme](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/World_Food_Programme "World Food Programme") and various economic analysts have warned that the war is driving significant, long-term increases in global [food prices](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Food_prices "Food prices").[749][750] A near-total halt of tanker traffic in the Strait of Hormuz has disrupted the supply of fuel and essential [fertilizers](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fertilizer "Fertilizer"), threatening global [food security](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Food_security "Food security") and echoing the [2022 food crisis](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2022%E2%80%932023_global_food_crises "2022–2023 global food crises").[751][752] The Fertilizer Institute (TFI) stated that nearly 50% of global [urea](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Urea "Urea") and [sulfur](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sulfur "Sulfur") exports, as well as 20% of global liquefied natural gas (LNG)—a key feedstock for [nitrogen fertilizers](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nitrogen_fertilizer "Nitrogen fertilizer")—transit through the Strait of Hormuz.[753]
#### Airspace closures
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/4b/Grounded_airliners_at_Manchester_Airport_Feb_2026.jpg/250px-Grounded_airliners_at_Manchester_Airport_Feb_2026.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Grounded_airliners_at_Manchester_Airport_Feb_2026.jpg)Airliners of [Qatar Airways](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Qatar_Airways "Qatar Airways"), [Kuwait Airways](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kuwait_Airways "Kuwait Airways") and [Emirates](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Emirates_\(airline\) "Emirates \(airline\)") grounded at [Manchester Airport](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Manchester_Airport "Manchester Airport"), 28 February 2026.
Iran's airspace was largely empty of civilian aircraft following the strikes as regional states closed airspace.[754][755] Bahrain, Iraq, Israel, Kuwait, Qatar, Syria and the UAE closed their respective airspace following the attacks, with multiple [airliners](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airliner "Airliner") being redirected to other destinations.[756][757][758] Some regional-based and International airlines suspended services to the Middle East in view of the war.[759] According to a _[Wirtschaftswoche](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wirtschaftswoche "Wirtschaftswoche")_ analysis, prolonging the conflict would mean a "catastrophe" for [Gulf states](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Arab_states_of_the_Persian_Gulf "Arab states of the Persian Gulf") such as Qatar and the UAE.[760]
#### Strait of Hormuz
Main article: [2026 Strait of Hormuz crisis](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Strait_of_Hormuz_crisis "2026 Strait of Hormuz crisis")
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/5a/Hormuz_map.png/250px-Hormuz_map.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Hormuz_map.png)The [Strait of Hormuz](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Strait_of_Hormuz "Strait of Hormuz") highlighted by a red box, at the entrance to the Persian Gulf
The current soft closure in Hormuz, alongside the fears of a declared blockade, has caused an increase in [financial risk](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Financial_risk "Financial risk") due to the impact in the prices for energy, shipping, insurance, aviation, etc. The war has provoked [speculation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Speculation "Speculation") in the [world economy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/World_economy "World economy"), caused by the possibility of prolonged supply disruption, which will have broader consequences for [inflation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Inflation "Inflation"), currencies, and emerging markets. One-fifth of global petroleum liquids consumption and one-quarter of global seaborne oil trade is done by the extraction of oil in the [Persian Gulf Basin](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Persian_Gulf_Basin "Persian Gulf Basin") and its passage through the [Strait of Hormuz](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Strait_of_Hormuz "Strait of Hormuz").[761] Mojtaba Khamenei, the new Supreme Leader, said the Strait of Hormuz would continue to be closed to pressure the US.[762] However, Iran said that ships can pass through the Hormuz as long as they cooperate with the [Iranian navy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iranian_navy "Iranian navy").[762] Araghchi said the Hormuz would be open overall but closed to Iran's enemies.[636]
#### Strait of Bab-el-Mandeb
See also: [Red Sea crisis](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Red_Sea_crisis "Red Sea crisis")
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f4/2023_Israel%E2%80%93Hamas_war_-_Bab-el-Mandeb.svg/250px-2023_Israel%E2%80%93Hamas_war_-_Bab-el-Mandeb.svg.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:2023_Israel%E2%80%93Hamas_war_-_Bab-el-Mandeb.svg)Map of [Houthi attacks on commercial ships](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Houthi_attacks_on_commercial_vessels "Houthi attacks on commercial vessels") from various countries in the [Bab el-Mandeb](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bab_el-Mandeb "Bab el-Mandeb") Strait as of December 2023
[Houthi-controlled Yemen](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Houthi-controlled_Yemen "Houthi-controlled Yemen") restarted its threats in the [Gulf of Aden](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gulf_of_Aden "Gulf of Aden") and [Red Sea](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Red_Sea "Red Sea"), which had been suspended since 10 October 2025 after the [Gaza peace plan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gaza_peace_plan "Gaza peace plan") proposal.[763] Some security sources claim that Houthis are planning to do incursions in the [Arabian Sea](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Arabian_Sea "Arabian Sea") and also use bases from the African side of the Red Sea, helped by their presence and some cells established in [East Africa](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/East_Africa "East Africa").[764]
[Hans Grundberg](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hans_Grundberg "Hans Grundberg"), the [United Nations Special Envoy for Yemen](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Office_of_the_Special_Envoy_of_the_Secretary-General_for_Yemen "Office of the Special Envoy of the Secretary-General for Yemen"), stressed the importance of protecting Yemen from being dragged into a new cycle of regional confrontations and urged the Houthis to refrain from escalatory actions, saying that "No party has the right to drag the country into a broader conflict that would bring more suffering to the Yemeni people."[765][766]
As of 4 March, hostilities had not resumed as there was reportedly internal debate inside the group over its response.[767] On 5 March, Houthi leader [Abdul-Malik al-Houthi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Abdul-Malik_al-Houthi "Abdul-Malik al-Houthi") said in a recorded address that his group stood with Iran in the ongoing war and was prepared to take action if developments required it, stating, "Our hands are on the trigger whenever developments require it", and warning that the group remains ready to intervene if the situation escalates further.[768][769]
By 7 March, the Houthis had released only three declarations, with a tone characterized as more disciplined and subdued this time, unlike its usual [bellicose](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bellicose "Bellicose") approach on other conflicts of the [Middle Eastern crisis](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Middle_Eastern_crisis_\(2023%E2%80%93present\) "Middle Eastern crisis \(2023–present\)"), seeming more as political and emotional statements in favour of Iran rather than practical and military solidarity. Such pragmatist attitude has been influenced by the [2025 United States–Houthi ceasefire](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2025_United_States%E2%80%93Houthi_ceasefire "2025 United States–Houthi ceasefire"), not wanting to open multiple fronts in the current [Yemeni civil war](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yemeni_civil_war_\(2014%E2%80%93present\) "Yemeni civil war \(2014–present\)"), which has been waging since 2014 and to focus on ending the conflict with [Saudi Arabia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saudi-led_intervention_in_the_Yemeni_civil_war "Saudi-led intervention in the Yemeni civil war").[770][771]
On 15 March, the _[Associated Press](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Associated_Press "Associated Press")_ reported that the weapons stockpile of the Houthis is running low after repeated attacks on Israel during the [Gaza war](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gaza_war "Gaza war"), while Iran's ongoing conflict has further disrupted weapons supplies, though the group retains a significant stockpile of drones. Despite these limitations, the Houthis have been recruiting fighters, expanding local weapons production, and reinforcing positions along the Red Sea coast, including the port city of [Hodeidah](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hodeidah "Hodeidah"), signaling potential escalation. Unlike Hezbollah's immediate intervention, the Houthis appear to act according to Iran's gradual deployment strategy, likely intervening only if the conflict widens or if Iran faces a serious threat to its military capabilities.[772][773]
On 16 March, _[The Times](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Times "The Times")_ reported that the Houthis are waiting for an approval from Iran to resume their attacks should Iran's control of the Strait of Hormuz be weakened by the US. Recent statistics indicate that nearly 30 oil tankers near the Saudi port city of [Yanbu](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yanbu "Yanbu") are currently within the Houthis' attack range.[774] On 20 March, senior Houthi politburo member Mohammed al-Bukhaiti declared that "All options at the military level are possible"[775][ _[better  source needed](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability#Questionable_sources "Wikipedia:Verifiability")_] and mentioned that the group was considering a [naval blockade](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Naval_blockade "Naval blockade") and would specifically target vessels belonging to "aggressor countries" involved in military actions against "Iran, Lebanon, Palestine and Iraq", to support Iran's ongoing "confrontation with the United States and Israel."[776]
#### Internet infrastructure
Further information: [Cyberwarfare during the 2026 Iran war](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cyberwarfare_during_the_2026_Iran_war "Cyberwarfare during the 2026 Iran war")
Three [Amazon Web Services](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Amazon_Web_Services "Amazon Web Services") data centers in the United Arab Emirates were struck and damaged as a result of [drone strikes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Drone_strikes "Drone strikes"), leading to outages of web infrastructure within the Middle East,[777] causing "sparks and fire" as well as "major structural damage" for 2 of the data centers, with internal water levels reaching up to 4 cm, alongside the mechanical failure of cooling and air systems.[778] As the targeted data centers within the United Arab Emirates were in different [availability zones](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Availability_zone "Availability zone"), and [Amazon](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Amazon_\(company\) "Amazon \(company\)") infrastructure was only designed to withstand redundancy of a single zone,[779] fundamental web infrastructure including [S3 storage](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Amazon_S3 "Amazon S3"), [EC2 compute](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Amazon_Elastic_Compute_Cloud "Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud") and [DynamoDB databases](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Amazon_DynamoDB "Amazon DynamoDB") suffered complete outages within the Middle Eastern region.[778]
#### Military costs
On 2 March, four days into the war, the [Center for American Progress](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Center_for_American_Progress "Center for American Progress") already estimated the cost to the US at $5 billion.[780] Analysts for CNN said that the cost was at least US$890 million per day.[781] The first 6 days of the offensive cost the United States US$12.7 billion the [Center for Strategic and International Studies](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Center_for_Strategic_and_International_Studies "Center for Strategic and International Studies") (CSIS) estimated.[127] with munitions alone costing at least US$11.3 billion, per the Pentagon.[782] This munitions-only cost is equivalent to over US$1.8 billion per day or US$1.3 million per minute.[128] By 19 March, twenty-one days into the war, _[The Guardian](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Guardian "The Guardian")_ extrapolated this cost to likely be over US$18 billion.[127] The same day, the Pentagon requested US$200 billion to fund the war, and a Harvard economist [Linda Bilmes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Linda_Bilmes "Linda Bilmes") estimated the total future cost of the war to the US government at over US$1 trillion, with US$600 billion spent on future medical costs for troops, assuming a similar total medical cost to the [Gulf War](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gulf_War "Gulf War").[128]
On 11 March, _[The New York Times](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_New_York_Times "The New York Times")_ reported that Iranian attacks caused damage to at least 17 US sites in the Middle East.[783] On 19 March, A US [F-35](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/F-35 "F-35") was damaged by Iranian fire during a combat mission over Iran. The aircraft made an emergency landing at a US airbase in the Middle East.[466] IRGC published a footage of the shooting.[784] In comparison, the monetary costs of Iranian attacks have been much lower than the costs of neutralizing them: PATRIOT and THAAD interceptors are US$4 million and US$12 million, respectively, as opposed to US$50,000 for a Shahed drone.[406]
## Reactions
Further information: [Reactions to the 2026 Iran war](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reactions_to_the_2026_Iran_war "Reactions to the 2026 Iran war") and [Media coverage of the 2026 Iran war](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Media_coverage_of_the_2026_Iran_war "Media coverage of the 2026 Iran war")
### Iran
#### Government
The [Supreme National Security Council](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Supreme_National_Security_Council "Supreme National Security Council") said Iran was targeted by a "brutal air operation" carried out by the US and Israel, saying: "This occurred once again during negotiations, and the enemy imagines that the resilient Iranian nation will surrender to their petty demands through these cowardly actions."[664] [Abbas Araghchi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Abbas_Araghchi "Abbas Araghchi") called the attacks "wholly unprovoked, illegal, and illegitimate".[785] An IRGC general announced that Iran is capable of fighting for a long time.[786] Iranian officials have rejected Trump's attempt to insert himself into the government's succession plans.[787] Pezeshkian said that US should take their unconditional surrender demand "to their grave".[788]
Pezeshkian apologized for the attacks on some neighboring nations, stating that Tehran would suspend them until an attack originated from there and attributed the strikes to "miscommunication in the ranks".[788][374] At a later date, Pezeshkian said that Iran has no choice but to target other Muslim countries if their territory is being used to conduct the war against Iran.[789] On 15 March, the Revolutionary Guard said that it would try to assassinate Netanyahu.[790]
On 19 March, [Esmail Baghaei](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Esmail_Baghaei "Esmail Baghaei"), the [Spokesperson for the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Iran](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Spokesperson_for_the_Ministry_of_Foreign_Affairs_of_Iran "Spokesperson for the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Iran"), accused the US and Israel of "terrorist acts" by assassinating top figures in Iran in an "illegal war". He said that Australia's military assets in the Gulf were legitimate targets for Iran's self-defence, after the [Australian Government](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Australian_Government "Australian Government") had deployed a [surveillance aircraft](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Surveillance_aircraft "Surveillance aircraft") along with personnel and defensive [air-to-air missiles](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Air-to-air_missile "Air-to-air missile") to the UAE.[791][792] On 20 March, [Abolfazl Shekarchi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Abolfazl_Shekarchi "Abolfazl Shekarchi") issued an escalatory warning aimed at U.S. and Israeli military and political personnel involved in the strike against Iran. In a statement released via state media, he claimed Iran was tracking the movements of "officials, commanders, and pilots" beyond active conflict zones and warned that public and recreational locations would no longer be safe for them. Analysts interpreted this as a signal of Iran's intent to expand asymmetric "shadow war" tactics beyond the battlefield.[793] Iran denied targeting Turkey and Oman, saying that they were "Israeli false flag" attacks.[96]
#### Civilians
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/54/Funeral_of_the_martyrs_of_Minab_Primary_School_for_Girls_6.jpg/250px-Funeral_of_the_martyrs_of_Minab_Primary_School_for_Girls_6.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Funeral_of_the_martyrs_of_Minab_Primary_School_for_Girls_6.jpg)Funeral procession for victims of the [airstrike on the Minab girls' school](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Minab_school_airstrike "2026 Minab school airstrike"), Iran, 3 March 2026
A holistic picture of civilian reactions in Iran is difficult to obtain due to the [government's control over the circulation of information](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Censorship_in_Iran "Censorship in Iran"), the [renewed internet blackout](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Internet_blackout_in_Iran#Renewed_blackout "2026 Internet blackout in Iran"), and the ongoing heated nature of the war.[794] In the initial aftermath of the strikes, there was evidence of both celebration and mourning at the killing of Iranian leadership.[795] Photos and videos showed people inside Iran celebrating the attacks, hopeful that they will bring forth an end to the ruling government,[796][797] as well as pro-government rallies with participants waving the [Islamic Republic flag](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Flag_of_Iran "Flag of Iran") and holding portraits of Ali Khamenei, while demonstrating against the US and Israeli attack.[798][799][800] In multiple instances, videos showed Iranians conducting protests against the government from the windows of their homes.[801][802] There was no immediate evidence of an uprising against the government.[795]
During the 17 March [Chaharshanbe Suri](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chaharshanbe_Suri "Chaharshanbe Suri") festivities ahead of the [Nowruz](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nowruz "Nowruz") holiday, many Iranians defied orders from the authorities and celebrated in the streets, with footage showing security forces firing gunshots to disperse crowds.[803][804] Civilians also expressed fear, grief and anger at the US-Israeli strikes, and at civilian casualties caused by them.[805][806] Some civilians who had opposed the Islamic Republic and supported regime change later recoiled at the prolonged and violent nature of the war. An Iranian sociologist said that the war was causing a [rally around the flag effect](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rally_around_the_flag_effect "Rally around the flag effect").[807]
#### Diaspora and opposition
See also: [2026 Iranian diaspora protests § Celebrations following the Israeli–United States strikes on Iran](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iranian_diaspora_protests#Celebrations_following_the_Israeli–United_States_strikes_on_Iran "2026 Iranian diaspora protests")
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/25/Iran_pro-Pahlavi_march_in_New_York_City_3%2C_March_1_2026.jpg/250px-Iran_pro-Pahlavi_march_in_New_York_City_3%2C_March_1_2026.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Iran_pro-Pahlavi_march_in_New_York_City_3,_March_1_2026.jpg)Iranian diaspora in the US celebrated the death of Khamenei in [New York City](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_York_City "New York City") on 1 March 2026.[808] Many fly variants of the [Lion and Sun flag](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lion_and_Sun_flag "Lion and Sun flag").
Following the US–Israeli airstrikes on Iran, some of the [Iranian diaspora](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iranian_diaspora "Iranian diaspora") showed support for regime change in Iran[809] and framed the war as a liberation rather than an invasion.[810] [Celebratory rallies](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iranian_diaspora_protests#Following_the_Israeli–United_States_strikes_on_Iran "2026 Iranian diaspora protests") were held worldwide by many members of the Iranian diaspora, where Iranians gathered waving anti-Islamic Republic symbols, including the [Lion and Sun flag](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lion_and_Sun_flag "Lion and Sun flag").[811][812][813]
[Reza Pahlavi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reza_Pahlavi "Reza Pahlavi"), the most prominent leader of the [opposition](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iranian_opposition "Iranian opposition") and son of the former [Shah of Iran](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shah_of_Iran "Shah of Iran"), expressed support for strikes targeting government and military sites, while also urging that civilians not be harmed. He called on members of Iran's military and security forces to stand with the public rather than the ruling authorities, and urged Iranians to prepare for the resumption of the [2025–2026 Iranian protests](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2025%E2%80%932026_Iranian_protests "2025–2026 Iranian protests") as the Islamic Republic was "collapsing."[814] He stated that he is prepared for [his proposed transitional government](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iran_Prosperity_Project "Iran Prosperity Project") to take over should the Islamic Republic be overthrown.[815][816]
Following the death of Khamenei, he said in an interview with the program _[60 Minutes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/60_Minutes "60 Minutes")_ that Iranians should be ready to take to the streets at an appropriate moment to advance political change. He argued that the ongoing attacks on the government had created what he described as "a real opportunity" for Iranians, saying that the public was prepared to "fight to end the Islamic Republic." Pahlavi also stated that he did not intend to seek political office or restore the [monarchy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Monarchism_in_Iran "Monarchism in Iran"), but aimed instead to help guide Iran through a peaceful transition to a democratic system.[814] Kurdish Iranian dissidents in Iraq denied any attack plans, but said they would support a US invasion of Iran.[817] On 7 March, an Iranian Kurd leader in Iraq said that ground operation into Iran is "highly likely".[818]
### United States
See also: [False or misleading statements by Donald Trump (second term) § Claims about Iran](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/False_or_misleading_statements_by_Donald_Trump_\(second_term\)#Claims_about_Iran "False or misleading statements by Donald Trump \(second term\)")
[](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:President_Donald_J._Trump_on_the_United_States_Military_Major_Combat_Operations_in_Iran.webm "Play video")[](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:President_Donald_J._Trump_on_the_United_States_Military_Major_Combat_Operations_in_Iran.webm)Trump falsely states that Iran was involved in the [attack on the USS Cole](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Attack_on_the_USS_Cole "Attack on the USS Cole"), 28 February 2026
President Trump described the attacks on Iran as "a massive and ongoing operation to prevent this very wicked, radical dictatorship from threatening America".[819] He said that "For 47 years, the Iranian regime has chanted '[Death to America](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Death_to_America "Death to America")' and waged an unending campaign of bloodshed and mass murder, targeting the United States, our troops and the innocent people in many, many countries."[820] Trump also claimed that attacking Iran avoided a [Third World War](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Third_World_War "Third World War").[636] Trump said that talks with Iran will be easier following Khamenei's assassination.[235] On the third day of the war, Trump said that the US military is "knocking the crap out of Iran but the big wave of attacks yet to come" and mentioning the possible use of ground troops.[235] According to experts, the use of ground troops are required for the goals of the Trump administration in the Middle East.[821]
On 3 March, Trump said on Truth Social: "Their air defense, Air Force, Navy, and Leadership is gone. They want to talk. I said, 'Too Late!'"[585] Trump claimed victory and said that he had defeated Iran militarily.[337] Trump has also informally stated his goal for a post-war Iran to follow US directives on oil production as Venezuela does, following the [US intervention](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_United_States_intervention_in_Venezuela "2026 United States intervention in Venezuela").[822] On 7 March, Trump shifted the war's objectives from urging Iranian civilians to seize control to demanding the "Unconditional surrender" of the regime, while stating that he would play a role in shaping the decisions that follow.[823][824] Trump stated that regime change in Iran will happen, but "maybe not immediately".[825] During the war, top US general Dan Caine said that Iranian forces are not more formidable than expected.[826] US intelligence found that Iran did not attempt to restart nuclear enrichment after the [Twelve-Day War](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Twelve-Day_War "Twelve-Day War").[827] On 23 March 2026, Trump postponed US strikes against Iranian powerplants for five days, saying he had held "productive" negotiations.[828]
#### Public
See also: [Protests against the 2026 Iran war in the United States](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Protests_against_the_2026_Iran_war_in_the_United_States "Protests against the 2026 Iran war in the United States")
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/bf/No_Kings_Portland_Oregon%2C_28_March_2026_-_148.jpg/250px-No_Kings_Portland_Oregon%2C_28_March_2026_-_148.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:No_Kings_Portland_Oregon,_28_March_2026_-_148.jpg)Protestors speak out against the US government's involvement in the war in [Portland, Oregon](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Portland,_Oregon "Portland, Oregon") during [No Kings 3](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_No_Kings_protests "2026 No Kings protests"). 28 March 2026.
[Opinion polling](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Opinion_polling "Opinion polling") showed that most Americans were opposed to US military action against Iran.[829] _[The Guardian](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Guardian "The Guardian")_ found 27% of Americans were supportive of the US operation while 43% were opposed and 29% were uncertain.[830] NBC found 52% of registered voters were against military action, while 41% supported it.[831] Most Americans believed that Trump had not explained the goals behind attacking Iran.[596] Polls showed splits on the issue along political and demographic lines. Most Republicans supported the action, while most Democrats and independents opposed it.[829] A 22 March [CBS](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CBS "CBS") poll of 3,335 American adults reported that 92% believe it's important for the conflict to end as quickly as possible, four in five said it was important to make sure the Iranian people were safe and free, 73% said it was important for the US to permanently end the Iranian nuclear program, 68% said it was important to stop Iran from posing a threat to other countries, and 53% said it would be unacceptable to end the war with the Islamic Republic still in power.[832] The [largest civilian protest in American history](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_No_Kings_protests "2026 No Kings protests") occurred on 28 March 2026 partly in reaction to the War in Iran.[833] A Reuters/Ipsos poll conducted from 17–19 March 2026, found that 65% of Americans believed President Donald Trump would order a large-scale ground war in Iran, while only 7% of respondents supported such a move.[834]
#### Politicians
Some members of the US congress called for a vote on the [War Powers Resolution](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/War_Powers_Resolution "War Powers Resolution"), also known as the War Powers Act,[835] that some other members asserted could have effectively ended the attack on Iran.[836] [Mike Johnson](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mike_Johnson "Mike Johnson"), the [speaker of the United States House of Representatives](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Speaker_of_the_United_States_House_of_Representatives "Speaker of the United States House of Representatives"), called the vote "dangerous".[596] The resolution failed,[837] mainly due to Republican opposition.[838] Most House Democrats and two Republicans[al] supported the Resolution, arguing that the military actions go beyond the president's authority to make war.[840] Most Republicans and four Democrats[am] voted down the Resolution, believing the War Powers Resolution empowers the president to engage in limited armed conflict.[840] A similar resolution was voted down in the Senate, mostly along party lines.[842] National Counterterrorism Center Director [Joe Kent](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Joe_Kent "Joe Kent") resigned in March 2026, stating his belief that Iran posed no imminent threat and the war was started due to the [Israel lobby in the United States](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Israel_lobby_in_the_United_States "Israel lobby in the United States").[843] As of 19 March, the Pentagon requested an additional $200 billion in funding from the White House; while Republican lawmakers like [Ken Calvert](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ken_Calvert "Ken Calvert") voiced support, and Democrats like [Betty McCollum](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Betty_McCollum "Betty McCollum") demanded to see a 2026 general budget first.[844] Representative [Thomas Massie](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thomas_Massie "Thomas Massie") and former Representative [Marjorie Taylor Greene](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Marjorie_Taylor_Greene "Marjorie Taylor Greene") argued Trump started the war as a distraction from the [Epstein files](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Epstein_files "Epstein files").[845]
#### American media
Main article: [Media coverage of the 2026 Iran war](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Media_coverage_of_the_2026_Iran_war "Media coverage of the 2026 Iran war")
[Brendan Carr](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brendan_Carr "Brendan Carr"), chair of the [Federal Communications Commission](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Federal_Communications_Commission "Federal Communications Commission"), threatened to revoke broadcasters' licenses over their coverage of the war.[846] Some Democratic lawmakers interpreted this as a threat of censorship and retaliation against coverage of the war in the media.[847]
### Israel
Netanyahu said the goal of the strikes was to "remove the existential threat posed by the terrorist regime in Iran", stating that "our joint action will create the conditions for the brave Iranian people to take their destiny into their own hands."[848] Foreign Minister [Gideon Sa'ar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gideon_Sa%27ar "Gideon Sa'ar") said that military action against Iran was urgently needed "despite the significant risks involved", adding that "delay would have allowed the Iranian regime to reach a level of immunity for its nuclear programme, as well as to engage in the mass production of long-range ballistic missiles".[849] Minister of Science and Technology [Gila Gamliel](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gila_Gamliel "Gila Gamliel") said that [2026 Israeli legislative election](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Israeli_legislative_election "2026 Israeli legislative election") will likely be brought forward to late June or July to allow Netanyahu's bloc to leverage the war.[850] Opposition leaders supported the strikes.[851]
On 4 March 2026, Defense Minister Israel Katz disclosed that Israel had initially planned to strike Iran in mid-2026.[852] On 5 March 2026, Katz said that Netanyahu set an aim of assassinating Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei in November 2025.[853] Israel's army chief, [Eyal Zamir](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eyal_Zamir "Eyal Zamir"),[630] said that the war will likely continue for a long time.[854] On 18 March 2026, Katz said that Netanyahu and he authorized the military to assassinate any senior Iranian official with no further approval.[855] [Mossad](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mossad "Mossad") Chief [David Barnea](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/David_Barnea "David Barnea") told Netanyahu that Mossad could spark protests against the Iranian government at the beginning of the war.[856] A March 2026 survey by the [Israel Democracy Institute](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Israel_Democracy_Institute "Israel Democracy Institute") (IDI) found that 82% of the Israeli public supported ongoing military operations, including 93% of [Jewish Israelis](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jewish_Israelis "Jewish Israelis") and 26% of [Arab Israelis](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Arab_Israelis "Arab Israelis").[857]
### International
Further information: [Reactions to the 2026 Iran war § International](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reactions_to_the_2026_Iran_war#International "Reactions to the 2026 Iran war"), [Protests against the 2026 Iran war](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Protests_against_the_2026_Iran_war "Protests against the 2026 Iran war"), and [2026 Iranian diaspora protests § Celebrations following the Israeli–United States strikes on Iran](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iranian_diaspora_protests#Celebrations_following_the_Israeli–United_States_strikes_on_Iran "2026 Iranian diaspora protests")
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/76/Ilham_Aliyev_visited_Iranian_Embassy_in_Azerbaijan_to_offer_condolences_3.jpg/250px-Ilham_Aliyev_visited_Iranian_Embassy_in_Azerbaijan_to_offer_condolences_3.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Ilham_Aliyev_visited_Iranian_Embassy_in_Azerbaijan_to_offer_condolences_3.jpg)Azerbaijani president [Ilham Aliyev](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ilham_Aliyev "Ilham Aliyev") visiting the Iranian Embassy in Baku to offer condolences for the death of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, 4 March 2026 [](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Message_from_the_President_of_the_Republic_of_Cyprus,_Mr._Nikos_Christodoulides_following_first_Iranian_strikes_on_Cyprus.webm "Play video")[](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Message_from_the_President_of_the_Republic_of_Cyprus,_Mr._Nikos_Christodoulides_following_first_Iranian_strikes_on_Cyprus.webm)Message (in Greek) from President of Cyprus, [Nikos Christodoulides](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nikos_Christodoulides "Nikos Christodoulides"), following the first Iranian strikes on Cyprus on 2 March 2026
It was reported that European Union Commission president ⁠[Ursula von der Leyen](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ursula_von_der_Leyen "Ursula von der Leyen") supports a regime change in Iran.[858][859] NATO secretary general [Mark Rutte](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mark_Rutte "Mark Rutte") said that Europe is "supportive" of the US attacks on Iran, as Iran is a "threat".[860] He also said that he felt that the US "knows what it is doing".[861] The head of the [World Health Organization](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/World_Health_Organization "World Health Organization") condemned the attacks.[862] The US and Israeli attacks were supported at least in part by [Ukraine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ukraine_in_the_2026_Iran_war "Ukraine in the 2026 Iran war"),[863][864] Australia,[865] [Canada](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Canada_and_the_2026_Iran_war "Canada and the 2026 Iran war"),[866] Germany,[867] and several EU countries,[868] while being implicitly or explicitly opposed by Armenia,[869] [China](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/China_in_the_2026_Iran_war "China in the 2026 Iran war"),[870] Oman,[871] North Korea,[872] [Pakistan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pakistan_in_the_2026_Iran_war "Pakistan in the 2026 Iran war"),[873] [Russia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Russia_in_the_2026_Iran_war "Russia in the 2026 Iran war"),[863] and [Spain](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Spain_in_the_2026_Iran_war "Spain in the 2026 Iran war").[874] Germany later became a critic of the war.[875] The US-Israeli attack was condemned by a number of [progressive](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Progressivism "Progressivism")[876] and left-wing parties,[877][878] as well as trade unions,[879][880] [anti-war](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Anti-war "Anti-war") organizations,[881][882] and some [far-right](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Far-right "Far-right") parties.[883][884]
Iran's retaliations were condemned by Bahrain,[871] Cyprus,[868] Egypt,[885] India,[886][887] Kuwait,[864] the Palestinian Authority,[888] Qatar,[864] Saudi Arabia,[259] Syria,[889] Turkey,[627] the UAE,[864] as well as by several other EU countries,[868] and France, Germany, and the [United Kingdom,](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_Kingdom_involvement_in_the_2026_Iran_war "United Kingdom involvement in the 2026 Iran war") who issued a joint declaration.[890] The United Kingdom permitted the US to use British military bases for defensive purposes,[891] although Trump refused their offer send over aircraft carriers.[892] Portugal permitted the US to use [Lajes Field](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lajes_Field "Lajes Field") for defensive purposes,[893] and France sent its aircraft carrier towards the Mediterranean.[627] When asked whether it would join the war, Canada said that it "can't rule out participation".[894] On 5 March 2026, French broadcaster [BFMTV](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BFMTV "BFMTV") reported that France authorized American armed forces to use French bases amid the war.[895] There have been movements between Saudi Arabia and Pakistan due to the [Strategic Mutual Defence Agreement](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Strategic_Mutual_Defence_Agreement "Strategic Mutual Defence Agreement") between both countries, as it "states that any aggression against either country shall be considered an aggression against both".[896][897]
Mixed reactions or calls for peace were made by South Africa,[898] Azerbaijan,[869] Lebanon,[899][900][864] Ireland,[901] Slovenia,[868] and the Vatican City.[902] In addition to condemning both the US and Israeli attacks and Iran's counterattacks,[868] Spain denied the use of its military bases for US flights connected to a military offensive against Iran, leading President Trump to threaten economic retaliation.[903] In Pakistan, the [army](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pakistan_Army "Pakistan Army") was deployed to suppress the escalation of violence during [pro-Iranian protests](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pro-Iranian_protests_during_the_2026_Israeli%E2%80%93United_States_strikes_on_Iran "Pro-Iranian protests during the 2026 Israeli–United States strikes on Iran") in the [northern](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Northern_Pakistan "Northern Pakistan") cities of [Gilgit](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gilgit "Gilgit") and [Skardu](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Skardu "Skardu"), especially after Shiite demonstrators attacked on 1 March the offices of the [UN Military Observer Group](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/UN_Military_Observer_Group_for_India_and_Pakistan "UN Military Observer Group for India and Pakistan") at [Azad Kashmir](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Azad_Kashmir "Azad Kashmir"). Similar action was done in [Sindh](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sindh "Sindh") during the [attack on the US consulate in Karachi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_attack_on_the_United_States_consulate_in_Karachi "2026 attack on the United States consulate in Karachi").[904] On 11 March 2026, the [United Nations Security Council](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_Nations_Security_Council "United Nations Security Council") passed [Resolution 2817](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_Nations_Security_Council_Resolution_2817 "United Nations Security Council Resolution 2817"), sponsored by [Bahrain](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bahrain "Bahrain"), which calls for Iran to stop its attack on the Gulf states and condemned the strikes as a violation of international law. The resolution did not mention US-Israel strikes on Iran and Lebanon.[905][906] On 20 March 2026, the UK approved the US to use British bases to attack Iranian missile sites targeting vessels in the Strait ‌of Hormuz.[907]
## Analysis
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/ef/Op_epic_fury_timeline_100hrs_-_pentagon_-_mar4_-_straight.jpg/250px-Op_epic_fury_timeline_100hrs_-_pentagon_-_mar4_-_straight.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Op_epic_fury_timeline_100hrs_-_pentagon_-_mar4_-_straight.jpg)Map titled "Operation Epic Fury Timeline – First 100 Hours" used during press briefing at the Pentagon on 4 March 2026
### Legality
#### United States law
Many legal and military experts said that US actions in Iran are a type that require congressional authorization. They also noted the frequent use of the word "war" in Trump administration communication about the strikes, adding further credibility to the claim that the Trump administration had bypassed congressional war powers. Rubio said that the Trump administration had followed the [War Powers Resolution](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/War_Powers_Resolution "War Powers Resolution"), but questioned whether it was legally binding.[908] Multiple American presidents and administrations have launched wars or military operations without congressional approval,[909] however legal experts have argued that Trump's actions in Iran can be distinguished from prior non-authorized uses of force because the "speculative pre-emptive" justification for this war is comparatively weak when measured against past domestic and international legal rationales.[910]
#### International law
The 2026 US-Israel attacks on Iran are widely considered illegal under international law, as they violate the [UN Charter](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/UN_Charter "UN Charter")'s prohibition on the [use of force](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Use_of_force_in_international_law "Use of force in international law"), because the attacks fail to meet the criteria for [self-defense](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Self-defence_in_international_law "Self-defence in international law") and lack authorization from the [United Nations Security Council](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_Nations_Security_Council "United Nations Security Council").[911][122] There is no recognized legal basis for preventive war or forcible [regime change](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_States_involvement_in_regime_change#2026:_Iran "United States involvement in regime change").[912] The attacks have been, therefore, considered a [war of aggression](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/War_of_aggression "War of aggression"),[913][914][915] which is the supreme [war crime](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/War_crime "War crime").[916][917]
[Ben Saul](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ben_Saul "Ben Saul"), United Nations special rapporteur on Human Rights and Counter-terrorism, stated that Iran had not enriched uranium to the point of building a nuclear device, that experts agreed Iran did not possess a nuclear weapon, and that the case was nowhere close to being self-defense against an imminent attack.[911] Don Rothwell, a professor of international law at the [Australian National University](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Australian_National_University "Australian National University"), stated that the UN Security council did not pass a resolution to attack Iran and had no legal basis for justifying use of force.[911] The _Law Society Journal_ noted that striking Iran during active negotiations was against the principles of good faith outlined in Article 2(2) of the UN Charter.[918] According to _[The Guardian](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Guardian "The Guardian")_ , under international law Iran has the right to attack US and Israeli military targets after their attacks on Iran, but Iranian attacks against civilians and against countries not party to the conflict are illegal.[122] [The blocking of the strait of Hormuz](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Strait_of_Hormuz_crisis "2026 Strait of Hormuz crisis") by Iran was described as a violation of the [UN Convention on the Law of the Sea](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/UN_Convention_on_the_Law_of_the_Sea "UN Convention on the Law of the Sea").[919]
### Geopolitical implications
For broader coverage of this topic, see [American expansionism under Donald Trump](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/American_expansionism_under_Donald_Trump "American expansionism under Donald Trump").
Numerous critics and analysts identified the US-Israeli attack with a more open embrace of [expansionism](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/American_expansionism_under_Donald_Trump "American expansionism under Donald Trump")[920] and [imperialism by the Trump administration](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/American_imperialism#Trump_administration "American imperialism").[921][922] The US–Israeli attack is widely considered a "[war of choice](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/War_of_aggression "War of aggression")".[923] A number of US and Israeli officials, including Hegseth,[924] have made [religious justifications for the war](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Religious_justifications_for_the_2026_Iran_war "Religious justifications for the 2026 Iran war").[925][926] According to some analysts, the war has been a setback for Russian influence in the region. However, the analysts also noted that it may distract the [United States](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_States "United States") from the [war in Ukraine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Russo-Ukrainian_war_\(2022%E2%80%93present\) "Russo-Ukrainian war \(2022–present\)").[927]
The war has been described as having various benefits and drawbacks for Chinese geopolitical interests. The [BBC](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BBC "BBC") reported that the closure of the [Strait of Hormuz](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Strait_of_Hormuz "Strait of Hormuz") and the war in Iran may weaken Chinese interests in countries that rely on Gulf countries' oil.[928] US aggression in Iran played into Chinese rhetoric that frames China as "a pillar of stability in contrast to an unpredictable America." Analysts noted that China has interests both in Iran and in the Gulf states, and that Gulf states may seek closer ties with China after facing attacks from Iran because of their US-alignment. However, analysts also pointed to the loss of discounted sanctioned oil and the inflationary impact of oil scarcity as potential economic drawbacks to the war.[929]
[Gilbert Achcar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gilbert_Achcar "Gilbert Achcar") said that the US was practicing a modernized version of [gunboat diplomacy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gunboat_diplomacy "Gunboat diplomacy").[930] Gokay and Hamourtziadou connected the attack on Iran with a longstanding pattern of US interventions in the region. They also connected the attack on Iran in the midst of negotiations to the history of United States treaty-breaking during [westward expansion](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Westward_expansion "Westward expansion").[931] Saeed Shah wrote that many in the [Global South](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Global_South "Global South") do not view the war as having the moral purpose that the US and many of its allies asserted, and that they instead see the war as a failure of diplomacy, and an act of aggression against a weaker nation. International relations experts said that the erosion of international law was making nations in the Global South feel insecure, and more likely to support a coalition against US interests.[932]
An analysis from [Iran International](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iran_International "Iran International") suggests that [Saudi Arabia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saudi_Arabia "Saudi Arabia") and the [United Arab Emirates](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_Arab_Emirates "United Arab Emirates") may be positioning themselves for a more active role in fighting against Iran.[933] The quicker Iranian response relative to that of the [Twelve-Day War](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Twelve-Day_War "Twelve-Day War") suggests a change in Iran's command structure.[934][935][253][936] [Reuters](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reuters "Reuters") said that by sending missiles at Gulf states, Iran caused them to realize that Iran poses a threat to them, and could thus cause them to support the US-Israel strikes.[937] Former CIA director [David Petraeus](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/David_Petraeus "David Petraeus") echoed these sentiments, stating that Iran's targeting of other Gulf states was likely a strategic error that could pull additional countries into the war.[938]
According to [Negar Mortazavi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Negar_Mortazavi "Negar Mortazavi") at the [Center for International Policy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Center_for_International_Policy "Center for International Policy"), the Houthis joining the war was "no surprise".[939] After the Houthis joined the war, Al-Muslimi, the [Chatham House](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chatham_House "Chatham House") researcher, said that the Houthis are capable of not only destabilizing the middle east, but the entire world.[940]
Based on the Israeli political landscape and enthusiasm for the war [Daniel Levy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Daniel_Levy_\(political_analyst\) "Daniel Levy \(political analyst\)") wrote on 30 March, that the nations interests may be opposed to all who are seeking an end to the war. Nonetheless he saw the Lebanon theatre of war as another campaign with no clear endgame. He concluded that " _Israel will continue to encourage and provoke escalation and to undermine any negotiation or ceasefire talks, including nudging the US towards ground operations._ " He saw Israels actions to actively accelerate US global decline.[941]
## Diplomatic efforts
On 25 March, Pakistani officials delivered a US-led "15-point proposal" to Iran, detailing a ceasefire plan to end the war.[942][943][944] According to Iran's English-language state broadcaster [Press TV](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Press_TV "Press TV"), Iran rejected the US proposal and outlined five conditions for an end to the war.[945]
